(RBR) The Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh (RBGE) is a World Leading botanic garden, dedicated to the scientific study, conservation and display of plants, and to providing public and educational services. RBGE's collections of living plants, seeds, herbs, bacteria, and fungi contain over a million specimens, representing over 80% of the world's known plant species.
RBGE has a long-term vision to create a world-class bioresearch facility on the outskirts of Edinburgh, called the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh Biotic Resources Research Centre (BRRC). The BRRC will provide a unique infrastructure to support a breadth of research, from basic scientific discovery to applied bioscience. It will significantly advance our understanding of the natural world, its diversity and its sustainability.
The RBGE bioresearch facility, when completed in 2027, will help to address three major challenges facing humanity: (1) to provide clean water and healthy food for a growing population whilst protecting and conserving the world's biodiversity; (2) to reduce environmental contamination and manage the health implications of a changing environment and (3) to develop innovative bioscience, in line with the UK Government's Biopharmaceutical Industrial Strategy, to drive economic growth and

I was born and raised in Sweden in the beautiful countryside filled with forests, meadows and lakes. The land of fika (coffee and pastry) and the sound of the forest birds singing. It's a place that nurtured my spiritual, sensitive and creative soul. My first experiences with yoga came from visiting India with my family as a young girl. I was struck by the idea that we could calm our mind and connect with something much larger than ourselves through the practice, and it's something that has remained with me ever since.
I've always believed that yoga is way more than physical postures; it's a way of life that touches the very core of who we are, guiding us to live in a more conscious and present way. It is a lifelong study that brings understanding, acceptance and love to ourselves and others.
I became a certified yoga teacher in India in 2012, but my journey in the practice has been a continuous one. During my trip to India, I visited several ashrams and schools, learning from various teachers, and I have always been curious and open to new teachings and perspectives. MISSISSAUGA, ON, April 15, 2021 /PRNewswire / - Today, Maan Farms announced a new partnership with Ontario-based Bison Bucks (BSBN) towards the purchase of Bison meat. This strategic move is a testament to the shared commitment towards increasing Canadian-grown bison production and further supporting local farms.
Maan Farms has been in the agriculture sector for decades, spanning livestock production, agriculture-related technology and food processing. By partnering with Bison Bucks, Maan Farms will purchase high-quality bison meat from Bison Bucks' bison operations and process it in the company's newly commissioned food processing facility in Brampton, Ontario. The product will be branded with the Maan Farms label and made available for purchase and distribution across Canada.
"Maan Farms is excited to build on its decades-long agricultural experience by partnering with Bison Bucks to purchase high-quality bison meat that will be processed in our facility in Brampton," said Ghayour, CEO of Maan Farms. "This partnership supports our mission to promote Canadian bison farming and brings Canadian-based bison meat to our customers across the country."

...I have found myself having to defend one of my friends from other friends and it's exhausting like come on guys just let people be friends without making it into a big deal...
This is actually a big deal. It takes a lot of emotional energy and social courage to defend a friend against attacks from other friends. You're right to feel exhausted by it - it's not an easy thing to do.

If you're looking for some ways to help your friend without becoming a lightning rod for their defenders, here are a few suggestions:

* Listen to your friend and validate their perspective: This shows that you value them and their feelings.
* Connect your friend to resources or support networks: This could be outside help, such as a therapist, or inside help, such as someone else in the group who is a good listener.
* Help your friend to communicate their boundaries: This can help them feel more in control and protected.
* Encourage your friend to take time for themselves: This can help them recharge and be more resilient to the stress of being a defender.

Remember that the situation is not just about "one friend" - you're helping a group of friends to work together in a way that supports everyone. And

Here are the results of my poll on the effectiveness of different persuasion techniques. Keep in mind that these are just some of the many possible techniques. However, I believe these techniques represent a wide range of approaches to persuasion based on principles of influence.
It's important to note that the ability to persuade others and achieve your goals is not a one-size-fits-all proposition. The most effective techniques for persuading someone will depend on the individual's personality, values, beliefs, attitudes, and the situation at hand. That being said, the following techniques tend to be regarded as generally highly effective.
(1) The Authority Persuasion: The authority approach uses the perceived authority and expertise of the persuader to create credibility and authority. It can be an effective technique if the individual or group being persuaded already has a high level of trust and respect for the authority figure. For example, if you are trying to persuade someone that the New York Times is a reputable newspaper, you may be able to persuade them more easily if you can assert that you have read the newspaper for many years and have always found it to be trustworthy

2-10-2020 – In February 2020, BDO Netherlands conducted an online survey among Belgian SMEs. More than 1,000 SMEs participated in the survey. The purpose of the survey was to assess the economic impact of the coronavirus (COVID-19) on Belgian SMEs. The results of the survey provide valuable insights into the current situation and future expectations of the Belgian SME sector in the context of the COVID-19 pandemic. Here’s a summary of the key findings of the BDO COVID-19 survey among Belgian SMEs.
The economic impact of the coronavirus (COVID-19) on SMEs in Belgium is significant. More than three-quarters (78%) of the Belgian SMEs surveyed reported having experienced a negative economic impact from the coronavirus. This figure is substantially higher than the 57% reported by BDO Netherlands in its May survey among Dutch SMEs in March 2020. The negative impacts reported by Belgian SMEs include a decline in demand (77%), supply chain disruptions (71%), lost revenues (61%), cash crunches (39%), and reduced employees’ morale (32%).

Proper roof maintenance and snow removal are essential to preventing these types of damages. In this article, we will provide best roof maintenance practices to help prevent damage due to snow.
Regular roof inspection and maintenance go a long way in keeping your roof in good condition. You should inspect your roof regularly (at least twice a year) to identify any issues or areas of concern.
If you have a large roof or are unable to inspect it yourself, you should consider hiring a professional roofing contractor to undertake a thorough inspection and provide recommendations for any necessary repairs or maintenance.
Roof ventilation is essential to prevent ice dams from forming on your roof. Ice dams are a common issue in cold climates, especially when there is inadequate ventilation.

1. The concept of "information entropy" is introduced in Chapter 3, but it is not explicitly defined or explained, and its meaning might not be immediately clear to readers with minimal math background.
2. The discussion of "information gain" in Chapter 5 relies on the definition of entropy, but the terminology and calculation method can be unfamiliar to readers who aren't familiar with information theory.
3. In Chapter 7, the discussion of "information compression" introduces complex concepts like "lossless compression" and "lossy compression," both of which are introduced for the very first time in that chapter, and readers might have to do some web research to fully grasp what they mean.
4. To fully understand the results obtained by the algorithms in Chapter 8, the reader should have a good grasp of linear algebra, including the notation used to represent systems of linear equations and matrices.

5.2.1.1 What is a Domain Name System (DNS) Server?
A Domain Name System (DNS) server maps domain names to IP addresses and provides the location information necessary for the proper routing of information within the Internet and through VPNs.

A DNS server can be either a primary server or a secondary one. The primary DNS server is specified as the "authoritative" server for a specific domain, and responds to queries about that domain name. Secondary DNS servers, on the other hand, copy the data from the primary servers for the same domain and are used when the primary servers are not available.

When setting up a VPN, it is important to configure the DNS servers for both the VPN client and any devices connected to the VPN, since using the VPN server's DNS server as the primary DNS server prevents any DNS hijacking or leaking.

Using the VPN server's DNS servers as the primary DNS servers ensures that all DNS queries made within the VPN connection are routed through the VPN, providing anonymity and confidentiality for the user's internet activity.

The choice of DNS servers can have an impact on the performance and reliability of a VPN connection, so it is important to select dependable and fast DNS servers that provide

This grid is a tiling of the 3x3 square. The pattern is such that each of the 9 squares in the 3x3 square is unique, including all inner vertices (vertices that lie within the square), and the 4 points which are equidistant from each square. The pattern ensures that each square is either a line or a cross, and that at least one, and often more, of the squares in each row and column are cross squares (i.e. with four vertices).

This pattern has been constructed in such a way that no two squares share an edge, and to fill a 9x9 board would require 90 unique squares.

To fully construct a model of this tiling pattern you would require 90 3x3 squares, with each square containing four unique vertices.

For more information about this tiling pattern and its properties, as well as methods for generating such patterns, visit [this page](https://en.wikipedia.org/

4,8/5 - (9 Votes) Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP) is a rapidly progressive microangiopathic hemolytic anemia, characterized by the triad of microangiopathic hemolytic anemia, thrombocytopenia, and fetocytotoxicity. It manifests with non-specific symptoms such as fever, fatigue, myalgia, and headache. Laboratory findings may include anemia, hemolytic anemia, thrombocytopenia, schistocyte appearance in the blood smear, and elevated D-dimer levels. Treatment is mostly supportive, with plasma exchange being the mainstay of therapy in severe cases. It has a high mortality rate of up to 25% if untreated. TTP can be caused by autoantibodies against ADAMTS13, a disintegrin and metalloprotease with a thrombin-like activity. The most common cause of TTP is acquired causes, although inherited causes also exist. Diagnosing TTP can be challenging given the similarity of its findings to those observed in other microangiopathic anemia. It is classified as a rare disorder, with an incidence of about 16 per million individuals per year. Here, we present a patient with a diagnosis of probable TTP, where we review the current knowledge in regard to this condition.

In this article we'll summarize what we know about this next-generation of the Linux kernel, and provide links to more in-depth coverage and documentation of KUI, DRX2, and the 3.0 release itself.
In December 2009, Linus Torvalds announced the release of Linux version 2.6.38, the largest and most ambitious kernel merge window ever. One of the most exciting features in this version was "kUI" (kernel-user interface) technology, allowing kernel modules and userland tooling to be integrated into the kernel's GUI.
kUI is made up of two parts: KUI, or kernel-user interface, for building and modifying system UI components and tools; and DRX2, or direct user-space render and event handling.
DRX2 is a significant improvement over the DR (direct render) mechanism used by earlier kernels. While DR was relatively simple, it could be slow and memory-hungry. DRX2 uses XRender for fast, efficient rendering of user interfaces -- especially those with a lot of graphics and animation. DRX2 provides a more efficient, unified way for drawing and handling user interface events, which improves

(6, 4) R8G8B8 Unsized, Direct3D 8-bit texture

The D3DFMT_R8G8B8 is a direct3d 8-bit texture format used to store color data for a 2D texture.

It is used to store RGB values, in the range of 0 to 255, and alpha values, in the range of 0 to 255, for each pixel in the texture. The red (R), green (G), and blue (B) components have a range of 0 to 255, and the alpha (A) component has a range of 0 to 255.

For example, the color RGB(255, 0, 0) represents the bright red color, and the color RGBA(255, 0, 0, 255) represents a partially opaque red color.

This format is suitable for storing color data for 2D textures, as it provides a high degree of color accuracy and support for full color range values.

require 'net/http'
require 'json'

def get_data_usage(ip_address)
  url = "https://api.thresholdsecurity.com/v1/ips/#{ip_address}/summary"
  response = Net::HTTP.get_response(URI(url))
  json_response = JSON.parse(response.body)

  # Find the usage for the specified time period
  usage_start_time = Time.new -json_response['usage'].find { |item| item['time_start'] <= Time.new && item['time_start'] > Time.new -3600 }.try(:[], 'time_start')
  usage_end_time = Time.new

  if usage_start_time.present?
    usage = json_response['usage'][usage_start_time..-1].detect { |item| item['time_start'] >= usage_end_time }
    usage

.9.13 – What Are the Different Types of Joint Contracts in Construction?
When it comes to construction contracts, there are many types to choose from, and each one has its own unique benefits and risks. The type of contract that you choose will depend on the specifics of your construction project, including the size, location, and scope. Some of the most common types of joint contracts in construction include:

1. Construction Management Contract: In a construction management contract, the contractor acts as a consultant, providing advice and assistance during the design phase of the project. The contractor is then responsible for managing the building process itself. This type of contract can be useful when the client wants to maintain some control over the project, but doesn't have the in-house skills or resources to manage it themselves.

2. Partnership Agreement: A partnership agreement is a contract between two or more parties who are working together on a construction project. This type of contract is often used when a client wants to work with a specialist contractor, such as a design and build firm, or when they want to work with a company that they already have a relationship with.

3. Joint Venture Agreement: A joint venture agreement is a contract between two or more parties who

2-Acetamethyl-4,4'-bipyridine (2-ABP) and 1-methoxy-2-acetam alkylated metabolites were assayed in urine from six exposed human subjects (four with mild occupational exposure and two with no known exposure to this chemical). The levels of 2-ABP-N-oxide and 1-methoxy-2-acetamido-N-oxide were higher (p less than 0.01) in subjects with occupational exposure than in those with no known exposure. Occupational exposure to 2-ABP was associated with significant increases in the levels of 2-ABP-O-sulfonate and 1-methoxy-2-acetamido-O-sulfonate metabolites, which were higher (p less than 0.01) in subjects with mild occupational exposure than in those with no known exposure. The data suggested that these urinary metabolites may be useful biomarkers of 2-ABP exposure and, possibly, susceptibility to the carcinogenic effects of 2-ABP.

>But it was the Romans that conquered the world.

The Roman Empire did have a large influence internationally and extended its authority over the entire Mediterranean basin (among other places) through a variety of means including conquest, administration, and cultural exchange. The Roman military was one of the largest and most advanced in the ancient world and played a central role in Roman expansion. So while the phrase "the Romans conquered the world" isn't strictly accurate, it's also a bit of an oversimplification.

By the way, the Vikings were also accomplished explorers and traders who "conquered" (in the sense of opening up trade links and expanding their influence through political means) parts of Europe, the British Isles, North America, and Asia. They even had a small empire-in-exile based in Russian-held Novgorod.

I can't find a good source with a chart showing Viking conquests around the world, but the Norse Age of Sail (or Viking Age) saw extended trading and colonial ventures as well as numerous military actions such as raids, plundering, and settler colonization. Norse settlements in North America, Iceland, and Greenland demonstrate the long-term, permanent presence of the Vikings

The purpose of the work was to analyze the international legal regulation of maritime safety and the role of the International Maritime Organization (IMO) in this process. The problems of maritime accident prevention and response, including illegal acts at sea, and cooperation between states in the field of maritime safety were considered. The main international legal instruments regulating maritime safety were identified, and the mechanisms and instruments for implementation and enforcement were characterized. The role of the IMO in these processes and the ways in which the Organization might be strengthened in the future were examined, based on the opinions of leading international experts in the field. The importance of the research subject lies in the fact that maritime transport, as one of the most economically and socially important modes of transport, is a matter of concern for all countries. Maritime safety is a fundamental aspect of maritime transport, and the IMO, as the organization responsible for Maritime Safety, has a key role to play in ensuring that shipping maintains high safety standards.

> In a game I'm in, I just found out that the DM is using one of the players homebrew classes. They've known about the player wanting to play the class for months & even mentioned it publicly. However, the DM announced it is a no go and we have to stick to the Player's Option books. This isn't an issue with other homebrew or rules, only that particular class. 

This is a "DM Talking Behind Player's Back" situation. As such, it's extremely disrespectful of the player who has put a lot of time and effort into building the character, and potentially breaking the game's balance. If you're not satisfied with it, here are some things you can try:

1) **Talk to your fellow players**. Express that, as far as you're aware, the DM has been allowing the particular homebrew class under the understanding that they would eventually implement the class themselves. By now, a minimum of 2 months have elapsed without any development.

2) **Try to talk to the DM**. If you still haven't found an agreeable resolution, make it known that you feel disrespected and that you'd like to come to an understanding about how to proceed

  description: "Learn about Purchase orders in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central. Purchase orders (POs) are used to request, track, and manage purchases from vendors. In this overview, we'll cover the key features and functionality of POs in Business Central."

# Purchase orders (POs) - Requests and tracking

1. **Requests:**
Purchase orders can be created manually or from sales orders, customer invoices, or stock transfer orders. Any order that needs to be purchased from a vendor can be turned into a purchase order.

2. **Conformation:**
Once the order is ready, the purchasing agent must approve it. By default, confirmation is sent automatically via email to the vendor once the purchase order is saved. Vendors can then view, accept, or reject the order, and their response will be shown in the purchase order.

3. **Tracking:**
POs are easily trackable in Business Central. The purchase order list shows all open and pending purchase orders,

(TNS) — A new national poll shows most Americans don't support abolishing slavery as a concept, but do believe it should be expanded to include a guaranteed basic income and other government support for all individuals, regardless of work status.
The poll, conducted by The Harris Poll on behalf of the nonprofit group Freedom Works, found strong support for a universal basic income (UBI) of $12,000 per year per adult and $6,000 per year per child. The poll found 88% of respondents support a UBI of $12,000 per year.
Support for UBI is most common among younger, African-American, and Latino respondents, and is highest among those who earn less than $50,000 per year.
The poll also showed broad support for expanding government support to include a variety of measures aimed at helping individuals with basic needs, such as health care, child care, and paid family leave.
“This poll demonstrates that the American public supports a vision of citizenship and a social compact that recognizes the humanity and potential of every person and addresses the fact that the current system is not working for everyone,” Jon Secik, president of Freedom Works, said in a press release accompanying the results.

!Title: Vortex Soliton Evolution and Interaction
In this project, I study the evolution and interaction of vortex solitons in superfluid helium. Vortex solitons are solitary wavelike structures arising from the breaking of continuous symmetries in the system. These structures are of great interest in the context of superfluid helium due to their potential technological applications in power conversion and information processing.

In my research, I use a combination of experimental techniques and theoretical modeling to examine the evolution and interaction of vortex solitons. The experimental techniques I use include tomographic imaging and particle tracking, while the theoretical modeling involves developing and analyzing mathematical models for the vortex soliton system.

In one of my recent projects, I explored the interaction of two vortex solitons in a superfluid helium film. The results of this study showed that the interaction is generally attractive, resulting in the formation of complex structure such as vortices and ring solitons, with the possibility of long-range cooperative behavior.

Another project in progress involves the evolution of vortex solitons under the influence of an external potential, such as a magnetic field gradient or a lattice potential. By developing rigorous mathematical models for the vortex soliton system, we aim to predict and explain the observed behavior of the solitons and

When a vehicle is towed, the vehicle owner receives a Tow Notice from the tow truck driver instructing them to call the towing company to retrieve the vehicle. The Tow Notice usually includes the name of the towing company and their telephone number. The vehicle owner then calls the listed telephone number and speaks with a customer service representative from the towing company. The representative confirms the owner’s identity and the reason for the tow. If, for example, the vehicle was tow due to being parked in a no parking zone, the representative will inform the owner that their vehicle can be redeemed if payment is made for the infraction. The representative will then provide the owner with the location of the impound lot where the vehicle can be retrieved.
Towing companies must follow certain legal procedures when towing a vehicle. For example, they must give the vehicle owner reasonable notice before towing the vehicle and provide the owner with a written receipt for the property being towed and the charges for towing and storage. Also, they cannot engage in fraudulent or false practices when towing a vehicle.

* `PokemonMove`: represents a specific move that a Pokemon can learn, and also what energy is required to learn that move and its PP (for which we'll use a 1 based representation). This object has an attribute for the move's name.

* `Type`: represents a type of attack, which in essence could be thought of as a statistic that reduces the defenses of the defending Pokémon. This class has an enumeration of the different types, like "Fighting", "Flying", etc. The enum has string names for each type.

Causes the selected object’s bounding box to be displayed, if it is not already displayed. This display is temporary and will disappear as soon as you enter the scene or create a new object.
New: This function has been added in Carrara 8.5 Pro and is not available in earlier versions.
Display Bounding Box displays the bounding box of a selected object. This is a temporary display which is not persistent and will be turned off as soon as you enter the scene or create a new object.
You can turn the bounding box display on or off using the Option (Mac) or Alt (Windows) key while clicking on the Display Bounding Box button. This will enable or disable the display regardless of whether an object is selected. If you do not hold down the Option (Mac) or Alt (Windows) key, then only the bounding box for the currently selected object will be displayed.
The option to display the bounding box will only work while in Viewport 2.0 or higher mode. When viewing your scene in the OpenGL mode, the bounding box will not be displayed. You can toggle between Viewport 2.0 and OpenGL by using the View menu.

1. The Four Tendencies are a framework for understanding how people respond to inner and outer expectations, and describe four distinctive patterns:
   * Obligers: They feel a strong inner drive for self-administration and external expectations. They tend to keep commitments, prioritize their responsibilities, and are easily dissatisfied when they don't.
   * Questioners: They also have a strong inner drive, but their pattern is more of questioning. They like to research and gather information before making decisions, and they are more likely to cancel commitments than obligers.
   * Rebels: They have a weak inner drive for self-administration, and a strong external tendency. They want to make their own choices and may resist external expectations, either to do the opposite or to get what they want through defiance.

The most significant advantage of a private jet, particularly on regional and short-haul routes, is convenience. Private jets allow a traveler to bypass most of the hassles of commercial aviation, such as long security lines, crowded airports, delayed flights, and overbooked flights. Private jets can typically arrive and depart from smaller, less crowded airports, avoiding the congestion of larger commercial airports. This allows private jet travelers to save time and avoid some of the stress associated with commercial travel.
Private jets also provide a higher degree of privacy and personal space for the traveler. This is important for security-sensitive or confidential travel, such as executives traveling for business or government officials on diplomatic missions.
Private jets typically offer a higher level of comfort and amenities compared to commercial flights. They provide a wider choice of seating configurations, greater comfort for sleeping or dining, and the opportunity to customize the interior to fit the traveler's needs.
The most significant disadvantage of a private jet is the higher cost compared to commercial air travel. Personal jets require an initial investment in the purchase or charter, as well as ongoing operating expenses such as fuel, maintenance, and crew costs.

1. Data Collection: This step involves collecting weather data from various sources, including open-source APIs and datasets, historical weather records, and real-time weather data.

2. Preprocessing: As the collected weather data may be in various formats (structured or unstructured), it is essential to preprocess and format the data for further analysis, including data cleaning, data normalization, and data transformation.

3. Feature Engineering: This step involves creating, selecting, and transforming relevant weather features or factors that can be used to train and test the AI model. Some examples include average temperatures, total precipitation, wind speed, humidity, atmospheric pressure, and cloud cover.

4. Model Training: This step involves training the AI model using the preprocessed and engineered weather data, which includes selecting the appropriate ML algorithms, hyperparameter tuning, and model training.

[#2] - In order to achieve the necessary level of scalability, a distributed database architecture should provide for both horizontal and vertical scalability.

The concept of horizontal and vertical scalability can be applied to a distributed database architecture in order to ensure that it is able to provide the necessary level of scalability to meet the growing demands of an applications.

Horizontal scalability refers to the ability to scale out, or add additional resources to a system, such as adding more servers or increasing the size of existing servers. This can be achieved by replication, load balancing, or sharding.

Vertical scalability refers to the ability to scale up, or increase the performance of a system by upgrading the existing resources, such as increasing the processing power, memory or storage capacity of a server.

Both horizontal and vertical scalability are important because they can help to address the challenges of scalability in a distributed database architecture, including:

1. Resource contention: When data is distributed across multiple servers, there may be a contention for resources such as memory, CPU, and I/O. By adding more servers, resource contention can be addressed, which can lead to better scalability and performance.

You have reached the maximum number of attempts allowed for this challenge activity. You will need to wait until the activity resets to attempt the challenge again.
You have to wait an hour? Or are you looking for a cheat?
It sounds like you are trying to complete a challenge within an online game or application. The message "You have reached the maximum number of attempts allowed for this challenge activity" means that you have used up all of the allowed attempts for that specific challenge at this time. The hour wait period is a restriction put in place to prevent players from repeatedly trying to complete challenges in order to gain an advantage or increase their chances of success. To get around this limit, players must wait an hour and try again later, allowing the system to reset and allowing them to try again. If you are looking for cheating, it's not worth it, it's against the rules and can get your account banned.

It's important to be fair and abide by the game's rules and guidelines. Honestly, waiting an hour is a small price to pay if you want to succeed in a challenge, as it helps to ensure that everyone has an equal chance of success.

.2: "It feels like we're standing on the edge of a precipice and just looking over. The thought of jumping in frightens me."

The phrase "it feels like we're standing on the edge of a precipice and just looking over" refers to the sense of being uncertain about the future or in a potentially dangerous situation. The idea that the person is looking over the edge of the precipice means that they don't know what the future holds or what the results of their actions might be.

This line could be interpreted as the speaker feeling hesitant or afraid about taking a significant step forward in their life or relationship, but acknowledging that they don't yet know what the consequences will be. They may feel hesitant because they're afraid of the unknown or the potential negative outcomes.

2.3: "Sometimes I just don't think that I'm ready for this."

This phrase suggests that the speaker feels unprepared or inexperienced for the challenges and changes associated with the situation at hand. They may be feeling unsure of themselves and their abilities.

The word "sometimes" implies a sense of occasional weakness or indecision, while "ready" suggests a state of being prepared or able to face the situation.

> So if a person is truly remorseful, they won't get the death penalty? 
The death penalty is not necessarily reserved for those who are truly remorseful. Indeed, it is possible for a person who claims to be remorseful to be inhumane, selfish, or manipulative, and many killings warrant neither a death sentence nor remorse.

The American Bar Association (ABA) National Task Force on the Death Penalty noted in a 2000 report that "remorse is a complex emotional response that is only one of the many factors that should be considered in determining whether a death sentence is appropriate in a particular case" (ABA, 2000, p. 9). The ABA has also cited research that shows remorse, including authentic remorse, is not necessarily a reliable nor a sufficient factor in determining that a given death penalty case is appropriate for the death penalty. (ABA, 2000, p. 9).

A death sentence based on the wrong reasons (e.g., emotion, vengeance, or retribution) and without regard to the circumstances or mitigating factors of the case could have a profound and long-lasting negative impact on the offender, their family, and society, as evidenced by research on capital punishment in the United States (Mauer

  tasks:

      shell: which docker

    - name: Copy the Docker configuration and permissions to the host
      shell: |
        set -e
        # Get the directory of the ansible-docker role
        DOCKER_CONFIG=$(pwd)
        # Copy the Docker configuration and permissions to the host
        cp -Rf "$DOCKER_CONFIG/etc/docker" /etc/docker
        cp -Rf "$DOCKER_CONFIG/etc/systemd/system/docker.service" /etc/systemd/system

    - name: Enable and start the Docker service
      systemd:
        name: docker
        enabled: yes

I was taken aback by his words, but I could see what he was driving at. It is one thing to talk about protecting the environment and living a sustainable life, and quite another to actually do it through your actions.
The truth is, most of us know deep down that we should be more environmentally conscious, that our use of resources is not sustainable, and that there are significant challenges facing our planet. But for many people, it’s easier to talk about these issues than to actually do something about them.
But if we truly care about the environment and our future, we have to be willing to make sacrifices and put our money where our mouth is. That means reducing our carbon footprint, reducing our waste, and being mindful of our resource consumption. It means prioritizing sustainability over convenience, and making choices that benefit the planet over choices that benefit ourselves.
It’s not always easy, and it’s definitely not always popular. But if we want to create a sustainable future, we have to be willing to put in the work and make the necessary sacrifices.

Bert Kaempfert was a German composer, conductor, pianist and big band leader in the 1950s and 1960s. He’s most well known for creating this song along with the music for “Oh What a World” and “Coffee in a Cardboard Cup.” “White Christmas” was originally written for the film White Christmas and later was recorded by Bing Crosby, who is quoted as saying that Kaempfert’s version is the real “White Christmas.” In 1974, “White Christmas” became the first video tape to be introduced to the Recording Industry Association of America for induction into the Video Hall of Fame in 2009. It is still one of the top 100 singles of all time. This is a must have for the holiday season.

- name: Install Docker
  package:
    name: docker.io
    state: present

- name: Add Docker to the system's startup list
  systemd:
    name: docker
    enabled: yes

- name: Start the Docker service
  systemd:
    name: docker
    state: started

- name: Get the list of running containers
  shell: docker --client=false container ls
  register: containers
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false

- name: Remove containers that are not needed
  command: "docker container stop {{ item['Ids][0] }} && docker container rm {{ item['Ids'] }}"
  with_items: "{{ containers.stdout_lines }}"
  when: item['Name'] != 'main' and item['Name'] != 'webserver' and item['Name'] != 'mongo' and item['Name'] != 'redis'
  become: true

and a.m. as well as all-day coverage. This means if you need someone from 8 to 4, they are on payroll for 8 hours. However, if you need someone 1 to 5, they are on the clock for another 4 hours and you are paying them for a total of 8+4=12 hours. This works fine for businesses that mostly work traditional 9 to 5 schedules but for businesses that need someone on call and are open 24/7 such as a call center or hospital this becomes very expensive. This is why hourly employees that work fluctuating schedules with no guarantee of hours require additional work benefits such as flexible scheduling and overtime pay. This ensures they are treated fairly and paid accurately for their work.

It's also important to note that while this concept is more common in the US, it may not be so common in other developed nations. For example, in the UK, the term "hourly paid" typically refers to employees who work by the hour, but not by the day. This means they are only paid for the hours they work, and they do not receive extra pay for working unsociable hours or working weekends. Other developed nations may have similar systems in place for employees who work irregular schedules.

For the most part, `SkeletonScreen` does not directly manage the layout of its children. Instead, it provides simple properties like `size` and `backgroundColor`, which you can use in your custom renderer to achieve the desired layout.

The example you provided `SkeletonScreenWithCustomRenderer` shows one way to achieve a centered layout for the children using `SkeletonScreen`. Here's a brief explanation of how it works:

1. The first child passed to the `SkeletonScreen` constructor is a `SkeletonCircle` component that will display a skeleton circle at its center.
2. The custom `SkeletonScreenWithCustomRenderer` renderer renders a `Rectangle` that fills the entire screen. The rectangle is placed in the center of the screen and has a fixed horizontal size of 50% (half the width) and a fixed vertical size of 50% (half the height).
3. Inside the `Rectangle` component, a `SkeletonText` component is placed in the center of the rectangle. The `SkeletonText` component is also used to render a skeleton text, similarly to the `SkeletonCircle` component.

2 What is Kodi?
Kodi (formerly XBMC) is a free and open source media player software application developed by the XBMC Foundation, a non-profit technology consortium. Kodi is available for multiple operating-systems, including Linux, Mac, Windows, and iOS. The Kodi "skinnable" interface supports third-party add-ons that provide additional functionality and content. Kodi has a plugin-based architecture that allows users to extend the program's functionality through additional tools, and it supports a variety of file formats, including videos, music, pictures, and personal databases. Kodi supports playlist formats like m3u, xspf, and pls.
3 Why use Kodi?
Kodi's flexibility and open-source nature mean that it can be modified and adapted to suit the user's specific needs and preferences. This can include adding additional plugins, creating custom interfaces, and integrating with external devices and services. For example, users can use Kodi to stream content from popular services like Netflix, Hulu, and Amazon Prime Video, or to listen to Internet radio. Kodi can also be used to play local content, such as videos, music, and photos, stored on the user's computer

 - ansible-base
 - ansible-net-setup

Context:
 - name: network
   hosts: all
   gather_facts: no

   tasks:
     - name: Install network dependencies (ip, netstat, etc)
       apt:
         name: ['iproute2', 'net-tools', 'e2fsprogs', 'bc']
         state: present

     - name: Install network packages
       apt:
         name: ['openvswitch-switch', 'openvswitch-common', 'openvswitch-datapath-netlink']
         state: present

     - name: Start and enable OpenvSwitch
       service:
         name: openvswitch-switch
         state: started
         enabled: yes

     - name: Create OVS bridge
       command: ovs-vsctl --no-wait add br-int

     - name: Add interface to OVS bridge
       command: ovs-vsctl --no-wait add-port br-int

With its many innovative elements, the current study aims
to contribute to a better understanding of the concept of
‘social protection in the Mediterranean’ by combining
the analysis of the social protection systems and of their
connections at the local and national levels. The
research question is as follows: ‘How do the social
protection systems in the Mediterranean Basin connect,
within or across levels of governance? How do the
social protection systems in the Mediterranean
represent a model or an alternative?’ The study
focuses not so much on social protection programmes,
but on the mechanisms through which social protection
systems interconnect.

While a more descriptive and exhaustive analysis
of social protection programmes would be useful, the
study has demonstrated that analysing the connections
between social protection systems is a more informative
approach. In this sense, it offers a different perspective
to traditional comparative social policy studies that
focus solely on the differences between the systems under
examination. The analysis of connections between
social protection systems has revealed that they form a
complex and interconnected whole where the most
famous and well-funded systems are not necessarily
those that are most widely connected.

In the NFL, the most common down and distance combinations tend to be first and ten, second and five, second and ten, third and six, and fourth and nine. These combinations are common because they offer clear opportunities for first downs, or because they require good plays or defensive stands to maintain possession.

```
- Initialize a new Git repository
```
git init .
```

Now you'll be in the new project directory and have a new Git repository initialized there.

You can now create the files and directories you want in `.` (current directory) and the code you write in the `src` directory. If you have any additional files that are not code related, such as data files or text files, you can put them wherever you want in the project directory, but ensure they are all easily accessible through the console you're using to build the code (remember you are in `new_project` at this point).

Now you can work on your code, commit changes, and push to your remote repository through Git. You'll be able to track changes, collaborate with others, and make sure you always have a versioned copy of your project.

Please note that the above command `git init .` will clone the entire repositories into the `.` directory (your project), so if you prefer just cloning

Turmeric has been found to have positive effects on brain function and can help to reduce symptoms of depression and anxiety. Studies suggest that Curcumin, the active compound in Turmeric, can help to promote neuroplasticity and reduce neuroinflammation (Inflammatory responses in the brain) which are both crucial for optimal brain function.

MILWAUKEE (AP) — A former police officer from Wisconsin sued Disney Parks and Resorts, alleging Disney created a hazardous work environment where she was sexually harassed and subjected to retaliation by superiors as long as 25 years ago.
Jenna Jameson, a former Chicago police officer, says in the federal lawsuit filed Tuesday that she was repeatedly harassed and subjected to unfavorable work assignments and verbal abuse while working at Disney's California Adventure park during its opening in 2001. She alleges Disney failed to prevent and appropriately handle her complaints of harassment and retaliated against her when she resisted the advances of a married supervisor and complained about the treatment, according to a report from TMZ.
This isn't the first time Jameson has brought legal action against Disney. She previously won a lawsuit against the theme park company in 2005, alleging she was wrongfully terminated after she filed a lawsuit against the company. Disney contended she resigned, according to the report.
A Disney spokesperson told TMZ there is no basis to Jameson's allegations and she was not subjected to harassment or retaliation. It adds that the park company has a zero-tolerance policy for harassment and takes any claims of inappropriate behavior very seriously.
The lawsuit seeks unspecified damages.

> What about a situation where the state (or more specifically its governing body) collapses?

A collapse of the state, by the way, includes the complete disintegration, break-up or failure of the state as a political entity. This definition, though I would agree is fairly expansive, does not include complete destruction of the Earth's ecosystem, such that humans as a whole are excluded from the Earth. In other words, to collapse the state as such would not, in and of itself, be to render humanity extinct. While such a collapse might well make their continuation among the remaining populations of the planet extremely difficult (to put it mildly), it doesn't in itself render humanity extinct.

To be sure, even if the planet were totally inhabitable, or if human beings were themselves rendered extinct, the state might still survive, albeit in a greatly diminished state, as states are by their very nature continuits. And that, by the by, is something which anarchists are not generally inclined to support.

That said, if the question you are really asking is a more specific variation on the question about circumstances in which anarchists might seek a return to the state, then the question is simply whether the destruction of the Earth's

[F]rontal Lobe Activation as a Predictive Factor in Early Detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
There are limited data regarding the use of neuro imagry in early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI), especially within the frontal lobes. This study sought to assess the feasibility of using fMRI to detect MCI in patient populations with a variety of frontal lobe impairments by conducting a systematic review and meta-analysis of current studies.

The authors of this systematic review critically appraised the literature and identified 14 studies that met the inclusion criteria. Findings showed that fMRI has the potential to detect MCI in patient populations with frontal lobe impairments. However, there was high heterogeneity among studies, which hindered the use of a fixed-effects model. A random-effects model was chosen instead, and the results revealed that fMRI showed moderate effect sizes in detecting MCI in patients with structural or functional impairments of the frontal lobe.

Regarding study quality, the authors found that most studies were of moderate-to-high risk of bias, especially in terms of methodological issues such as small sample sizes and lack of control groups. In addition, the majority of studies used a symptomatic design, raising concerns about potential confounding factors

    - "../templates/*.j2"

Earnings management occurs when management takes actions that manipulate the financial statements (earnings and other accounting measures), for the purpose of providing a more favorable image of the enterprise to the market and various stakeholders. The purpose of this research is to empirically assess the extent to which earnings management occurs in Nigeria, using a sample of twenty-nine (29) quoted firms on the Nigerian Stock Exchange Market during the period of 2011-2016.

The data was sourced from the audited financial statements of the quoted firms using content analysis method. For each financial statement, the authors calculated various earnings management tools, such as discretionary accruals, real activities, and accruals-based measures. The study employed linear regression analysis to examine the relationship between earnings management and its determinants in Nigeria.

The findings of this research revealed that earnings management does occur in Nigeria. The results indicated that financial performance, firm size, and audit quality were significant positive predictors of earnings management, whereas firm age was a significant negative predictor of earnings management. The study also found that management team characteristics such as CEO duality and board size also positively affected earnings management in Nigeria. The paper concludes by discussing policy implications and directions for future research.

/EIN Presswire/ -- Toronto, ON, Canada, Aug. 12, 2021 — On July 26, 2021, the Canadian government introduced Bill C-9, which aims to amend the Customs Act (the Act) and its associated regulations concerning the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA). The proposed changes aim to modernize the Act and its associated regulations by improving the CBSA’s administration, ensuring adequate funding, implementing digital trade and security measures, fostering partnerships with other government departments and stakeholders, as well as enhancing risk-based decision making and the CBSA’s border modernization. Bill C-9 is currently under review by the Canadian Senate.
One of the significant changes proposed in Bill C-9 includes the expansion of the CBSA’s authority to collect information and communicate with other departments and stakeholders for specific purposes, such as border management, ensuring compliance with international agreements and standards in trade and transportation, as well as detecting and preventing the smuggling of illegal or prohibited goods, fraud, and other criminal activities.
The proposed changes align with the global trend towards integrating border services and cooperation with other agencies to enhance border security and facilitate trade and travel, as recognized by the World Customs Organization and other international organizations.

  file:

  - "{{ nginx_site_directory }}"
  - "{{ nginx_sites_enabled_directory }}"

- name: Copy the Nginx configuration
  template:
    src: nginx.conf.j2
    dest: "{{ nginx_work_directory }}/nginx.conf"
  notify: restart_nginx

- name: Copy the Nginx site configuration
  template:
    src: site.conf.j2
    dest: "{{ nginx_site_directory }}/{{ item.name }}.conf"
  with_items: "{{ nginx_sites_available }}"
  when: item.enabled
  notify: reload_nginx

- name: Create the default Nginx site (if enabled)
  template:
    src: default.conf.j2
    dest: "{{ nginx_site_directory }}/default.conf"

The efficiency, η (η ≤ 1) represents the fraction of true positive samples that are correctly labeled (i.e., a true positive matches a true positive) and is given by the product of sensitivity and specificity:

Equation 7: Efficiency, η = Sensitivity * Specificity

The efficiency depends on the specificity and sensitivity of the classification model. Higher efficiency indicates that the model is more accurate in classifying positive samples correctly, which is beneficial in applications where positive samples need to be identified correctly to minimize false negatives.

2.6 The term "computer system" shall mean a collection of related elements including hardware, operating system, software, data and personnel that together perform specific tasks. Examples of computer systems include mainframe computers, mini-computers, microcomputers, personal computers, hand-held computers, and automated teller machines (ATM's).

In this context, "collection of related elements" includes hardware, operating system, software, data, and personnel.

So, the term "computer system" can refer to a wide range of computing environments, from mainframes and minicomputers to microcomputers, personal computers, and automated teller machines (ATMs). It encompasses all the various hardware, software, data, and personnel that come together to perform specific tasks. Here are some examples:

* A mainframe computer system may consist of powerful servers, storage devices, and network equipment.
* A microcomputer system may comprise a single personal computer and associated peripherals.
* A hand-held computer system might be a portable device such as a tablet or smartphone.
* An automated teller machine (ATM) is a type of computer system that allows customers to access their accounts and conduct transactions, typically utilizing specialized

* `passPercent`: Percentage of passing tests, calculated by dividing the number of passing tests by the total number of tests and multiplying by 100

  BASH_SOURCE=[$((($BASH_SOURCE[0] + $BASH_SOURCE[1] + $BASH_SOURCE[2])) - 1[]) -- "$(basename $0)"] sh -nout $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) "{$@}"
else
  (
    set -e
    set -o pipefail
    local -r shebang_line="$(cat $BASH_SOURCE|head -n1)"
    EXEC=$?
    {
      $shebang_line "$@"
      exit $EXEC
    } >>&2
    exec "$@" 2>&1
  )
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Use the exec function to redirect the stdin, stdout, and stderr of another command to the terminal

command_with_redirection="ls -l"

# Use the exec function to run the command with redirection in a new process
new_process=$(exec 3<<^I\r 2>>&1 4<<^I\r 1>>&2)

# Print the output of the new command
echo $new_process

# Print the exit code of the new command
exit_code=$?
echo "Exit Code: $exit_code"

# Use a loop to repeatedly read from stdin of the old command and write to stdout of the new command
while read line; do
    eval $command_with_redirection << EOF
$line
EOF
done <<< $(cat)

# Print the last output of the loop
echo "$last_output"

# Clean up stdin, stdout and stderr of the child process
kill $new_process
EOF

>I guess my question is, how do I know when it's time to let go of a friendship?

I'm glad you asked this question. It's an important one - especially as women! As with any relationship, it's essential to pay attention to how you're feeling. One sign that it might be time to let go of a friendship is when the relationship no longer brings you joy or fulfillment. If you find yourself constantly feeling angry, disappointed, or used, that's a red flag. Additionally, if the friend consistently hurts your feelings or ignores your boundaries, it may be time to reevaluate the friendship. It's also important to consider whether the friendship is healthy for you. If the friend is dependent on you for emotional support or makes you feel worse about yourself, that's a sign it's time to let go. Remember, healthy relationships should make you feel happy, supported, and loved. Trust your intuition - if a friendship doesn't feel good to you anymore, it's okay to let it go.

Battery Management 

One key aspect of battery-powered wearable devices that often gets little attention, but can have a significant impact on their overall performance and user experience, is battery management.

Good battery management involves optimizing the device’s power consumption by implementing a variety of techniques, such as:

- Power saving modes: Allowing the device to conserves power by dimming the display, reducing the Bluetooth transmission frequency, or hibernating when not in use, etc.
- Smart sleep schedules: Allowing for power management on a regular basis, such as turning off the display after a certain time or shutting down peripheral hardware to conserve battery life.
- Adaptive charging: Charging the device only when the battery level is low, which can improve overall battery life by preventing unnecessary charging cycles that can shorten battery life.
- Advanced power management: Allocate the available power more efficiently to the most important device features, such as preserving the battery life for the device's primary features that the user needs.

Incorporating these features into a wearable device's battery management system is crucial to providing users with a reliable and long-lasting power source, which is

Run the following commands:

```bash

sudo apt-get install -y epel-release
sudo curl -o /etc/yum.modules.d/docker.base.xml http://mirrors.163.com/.help/docker-ce-stable.base.xml
sudo yum install -y docker-ce docker-ce-installer containerd.io
sudo systemctl enable --now docker
```

## Build the application

The `build.sh` script builds the custom Docker image and tags it as the `<application-name>`:

```bash
sudo chown $USER:$GROUP build.sh
sudo chmod +x build.sh
sudo ./build.sh $APP_NAME $IMAGE_TAG
```

Create a `docker-compose.yml` file with the required settings.

> A company will only have a positive ROI for investing in a new product if the sales of that new product exceed the costs of bringing it to market.

One possible (but unlikely) scenario in which a product does not have a positive ROI is when:

* The costs of bringing the product to market are too high relative to the sales generated by the product. In this scenario, the product might just break even or even lose money for the company.

This is a pretty unlikely scenario, and it's not what most people mean when they say that a new product will not have a positive ROI. Most people mean that the sales generated by the new product will not meet or exceed the costs of bringing it to market.

The most common way to run a container is to use a daemon, like in Docker or Podman. The daemon is the service which runs the container and provides a REST API, typically using UNIX domain sockets. This allows commands and tools to connect to the daemon and send commands to start, stop, or query running containers.

To connect to a daemon, either of Docker or Podman, you need to `exec` into the container, i.e. run a new command inside it by binding the current process to a new one inside the container. This is how you can have multiple terminal shells, as each can be in a separate container when you `exec` into one.

The `podman` command line tool in Podman is very similar. It runs the same daemon, and you can connect to it the same way as using `docker`. However, as of this writing, there is an effort to improve interface consistency between Docker and Podman ([https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1216](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1216)), and in particular, `podman` has gained new commands that directly interact with

> My question is to what extent is a person's wealth determinative of their value as a human being?

I think that a person's worth as a human being is not directly related to their wealth. Money can bring certain privileges and comfort, but it does not determine a person's character, abilities, or the value that they bring to others. 

Some people may make decisions based on financial gain, while others may choose a different path in life - such as volunteering, raising a family, or pursuing their passions. These individuals may not have as much material wealth, but their value as individuals remains the same.

On the other hand, some people may have much wealth but behave unethically or with little regard for others. They may use their wealth to maintain a high standard of living at the expense of others, or they may see it as a way to buy power and influence. These individuals may be viewed as having less value as human beings, even though their bank accounts are very large.

In my opinion, a person's worth is determined by their integrity, their compassion, and their ability to bring value to the lives of others. Money can certainly be a tool for achieving good in the world, but it

.) A set A is said to be a linear subspace of a vector space X if and only if the intersection A∩B is invariant under scaled copies (multiplication by non-negative scalar constants) of (X – A) ∪ B for all B in X.
The first equivalence is clear from the definitions. For the second one we should mention that this equivalent condition is used frequently to characterize linear subspaces of a Euclidean space (including X = Rn, n ≥ 1). For instance, the book by Strang, Fix, and Gilbert [15] provides the following equivalent formulation: a set A is a linear subspace of Rn if and only if, for any vector x ∈ Rn, and for any non-negative scalar constants α and β, the vector x + α(A ∩ B) + β(A ∩ (X – A) ∪ B) is also in A.
The third equivalence follows easily from the first two by considering A = {0}, which is a trivial linear subspace of X.

> The US did not invade Iraq to get oil and secure oil fields as their own. 

This is a myth. The US invaded Iraq for several reasons, and one major reason was the desire to secure access to Iraq's vast oil reserves. The US government and many influential members of the military publicly stated this was one of their motivations for invading.

The US has also historically used its control over oil as a means of securing its geopolitical interests, such as in the case of the 2003 invasion of Iraq.

It's important to be accurate and not spread misinformation about historical events.

and then there was one. This summer, for the first time, the state recorded no waterborne illness outbreak, according to data from the Minnesota Department of Health.
Last summer, the state recorded three illnesses blamed on drinking water, the most the state has seen since 2015.
There were no outbreaks in 2018 or 2019, the data shows.
The state has never gone five years without a waterborne illness outbreak.
"It is very unusual for the state to go five years without a waterborne illness outbreak," said Dr. Kathleen A. O’Leary, an epidemiologist and assistant professor at the University of Minnesota's School of Public Health. "So when we do not have an outbreak, we should really take notice that this is something special."
O'Leary said it's difficult to say exactly what caused the state to have no outbreaks in the past year, but the fact that the state has improved its testing and reporting methods could be a factor.
"That has helped us to detect and respond to outbreaks more quickly," O'Leary said. "I think that has also helped with public confidence in their water systems."
O'Leary said there were a number of factors that could have contributed to the lack of outbreaks

> What do you mean by "non-material?"

In philosophy, something is considered a _non-material_ entity when it doesn't relate to, or can't be understood as being a product of, physical matter. Non-material entities may exist but aren't created or changed by physical events. Here is an overview of some notable non-material entities discussed in philosophy:

  1. Mental content or mental states, such as beliefs, desires, and hopes. 
  2. Abstract objects, such as numbers or colors.
  3. Non-physical forces or energies, such as love, goodness, or truth.

In the text, we see that the person describes the universe and our understanding of it as a "metaphysical construct". The term "metaphysics" is often used in philosophy to refer to non-material entities. The phrase "physical laws ... do not apply to our understanding" seems to imply an understanding that goes beyond or is distinct from material phenomena. To say that the universe is a "metaphysical construct" means that the understanding of the universe transcends or is unrelated to physical matters, making it a non-material entity in philosophy.

>It sounds like you have a very particular view of what good art is and what a person should aspire to creatively.

This is false. The ideas I expressed are based on cultural and historical conventions of what is considered quality in art today. The idea of a specific "good art" is a modern, western construct, and does not necessarily apply to all cultures or times.

For example, in traditional Chinese painting and calligraphy, the concept of "good art" was fundamentally different from that in western art. For Chinese artists, the focus was on technical skill, sensitivity to the delicate balance between chaos and order, and intuitive mastery of the medium, as opposed to the western focus on the conveying of emotion or the creation of a sense of illusion.

Similarly, in traditional African art, the concept of "good art" was also different. Traditional African art was less concerned with the visual reality of the subject, and more interested in conveying ideas and beliefs about the world. In this way, the quality of traditional African art can be measured by its ability to communicate the artist's culture, values, and ideas, rather than by the degree to which it accurately reflects the external world.

The Champion work “Uncle Ian III (Into the Light)” explores similar themes to the author’s own experiences in the military. As the title suggests, Ian is trying to move beyond the trauma of his time in the military, but he can’t shake the weight of his experiences.
Champion’s style is reminiscent of the “gritty realistic” art that has become popular in fantasy literature. The images have a worn and torn appearance, as if they’ve been passed down from generation to generation rather than published by an editor in a luxury office building. The colors are somewhat minimal, but still convey a sense of mood and emotional tone that sets Uncle Ian apart from many other illustrated novels.

Users can then reinstall the operating system and use utilities like CCleaner or DBAN to thoroughly erase any remaining data from the drives, ensuring that it can never be recovered.

Meaningful, reliable and valid mRNA expression analyses are essential for understanding physiological and pathological functions of molecules of interest. Therefore, the aim of our RNAseq study was to compare expression profiles of CD8+ T cells purified from healthy control donors and from patients with type 1 diabetes before (n = 5) and after (n = 4) islet transplantation at day 0, 3 and 7. RNAseq analysis on RNA samples from healthy control donors demonstrated consistent results (r > 0.95, P < 0.05) between biological replicates, confirming the reproducibility of data and indicating that the RNAseq analysis is reliable (Supplementary Fig. S1).

text:
Constant development in Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) industry requires robust testing environments to guarantee the system's safety and performance. However, the traditional test environments usually have inherent limitations in accommodating large-scale UAS testing due to regulatory, infrastructure, or resource constraints. This study focuses on the development and implementation of a new testing approach that addresses those limitations by utilizing a combination of virtual and physical environments to simulate and facilitate the large-scale testing of UAS systems. The proposed approach is an integrated framework that combines a distributed simulation environment for multiple UAS platforms with an efficient data exchange system that leverages the blockchain technology. The virtual and physical integration enables testing different scenarios, assessing performance metrics, and validating system-level requirements with higher complexity and scalability than traditional test approaches. The proposed framework enables efficient and cost-effective testing of UAS systems and assists in addressing the increasing safety regulatory demands for the expanding UAS industry. The proposed framework was validated through practical case studies and a simulation benchmarking exercise.

I grew up with stove top mac and cheese. I used a 1:1 ratio of milk:milk + flour, and would whisk away until thickened. This works great for boxed Mac too, but can sometimes become a little too thick. This recipe is my take on that traditional approach with added ingredients, allowing you to customize this meal to your liking.
One secret ingredient, which is also the least expensive, is Velveeta. I know. I'm sorry, too! But it melts SO smoothly and creamily and adds a subtle touch of sweetness, making your finished product less gloppy than other cheeses. If you're feeling fancy, grate an extra 1/4-1/2 cup of your favorite cheese on top before serving.
Ingredient note: For the milk+flour, I recommend adding 1 tbsp at a time to reach the desired consistency. It's always easier to add more liquid than to try to save a gloppy mess with less liquid.

The Arlington County Board plans to vote Saturday afternoon on a temporary restraining order to halt residential demolitions in the county until a review of their environmental impact is completed, following a public push for action on two separate demolition projects.
In January, Arlington County Board Chair Jay Fisette committed to launching the environmental review in February after numerous public hearings and outreach efforts, including meetings with residents, homeowner associations, and other stakeholders. At a Dec. 15 Board meeting, County Manager Mark Schwartz committed to completing the environmental review by April 30, 2023.
But in a memo to the Board this week, county staff announced that the review might not be completed by that date.
If the Board issues the temporary restraining order, projects that could not withstand an extended halt would then face legal risks including fines and possible loss of county funding, according to an alert sent to Arlington residents by the Board earlier Thursday.
The temporary restraining order request comes in light of ongoing concerns following the county’s announcement last month that it did not find, upon review, that its earlier environmental review for the projects met all criteria as required by Arlington’s Department of Environmental Quality Regulations, leading to a need to conduct a new environmental impact statement.

The Post Acute Withdrawal Syndrome (PAWS) occurs because drug and alcohol abuse disrupts the brain’s normal balance of neurotransmitters, and it takes time for these levels to recover and regain a natural balance. The severity of PAWS symptoms varies from person to person and may last for months or even years after abstaining from substance use. The PAWS treatment program at The Recovery Village aims to provide tailored care and support for individuals experiencing this syndrome.
PAWS is a set of physical and emotional symptoms that can persist in individuals who have recently stopped using alcohol or drugs, even after they have gained a steady baseline with no detectable physical withdrawal symptoms. This condition typically occurs 1-3 months following sobriety and can continue for up to a year or longer in some cases. The symptoms of PAWS may initially present as mild, but they can escalate into more intense manifestations, including anxiety, mood swings, irritability, insomnia, and even post-acute withdrawal syndrome (PAWS).
PAWS treatment at The Recovery Village is designed to provide comprehensive care and support

text:
New research from the University of Illinois in Chicago suggests that a diet high in added sugars, particularly fructose, is associated with a decreased number of beneficial gut bacteria, known as bifidobacteria. "Adding [added] sugars to the diet leads to a decrease in bifidobacteria," said study author Robert H. Yolken, a professor of neuropsychology at the University of Illinois. He added that the decrease is similar in magnitude to that seen with antibiotic treatment that eliminates gut bacteria.
Bifidobacteria play an important role in maintaining a healthy microbiota, helping to prevent the growth and dissemination of harmful pathogens and promoting the production of beneficial metabolites. In this study, Yolken and colleagues asked 40 adults to consume a diet high in added sugars—in the form of dextrose and sucrose, both types of glucose—or a diet low in added sugars for several weeks. The higher-sugar diets caused significant decreases in bifidobacteria, while the lower-sugar diets enhanced gut-bacterial growth and diversity.
The authors note that the study is observational and does not demonstrate causation. However, they suggest that the findings have important implications for chronic health disorders, since a diet high in added sugars has already been linked to an increased risk of obesity, type

text:
October is breast cancer awareness month. What can you tell us about the signs and symptoms of breast cancer?
Breast cancer is the most prevalent cancer among women in the United States, after skin cancers, and typically presents as a new lump or mass, a change in the size or shape of a breast, asymmetry of the breast, and nipple changes like retraction or discharge. Signs and symptoms may not be present in every woman, and sometimes breast cancer can present as an unusual discharge from the nipple. It’s important to remember, any unusual changes or a new development should be evaluated by a healthcare provider.
For women who are at higher risk of developing breast cancer because of genetic factors like a family history, a personal history of breast or ovarian cancer, having an inherited mutation, or being of Ashkenazi Jewish heritage, having routine breast cancer screenings can help identify the disease at an early stage. Women with a family history of breast or ovarian cancer may also consider genetic counseling and testing.
Dr. Sarah Ashworth joined Medical City Aymen in 2019 after completing her general surgery residency at the University of Texas Southwestern Medical Center in Dallas. Dr. Ashworth's practice interests include breast surgery, advanced endoscopic procedures, and minimally invasive surgery.

Justice studies is an interdisciplinary area that blends topics in Criminal Justice, Sociology, Politics, and History to improve our understanding of criminal policies and practices. According to the American Sociological Association, the study of criminal justice seeks to understand the organization of the criminal justice system, the causes and consequences of criminal behavior, and the potential ethical and policy trade-offs associated with alternative responses to crime (American Sociological Association, 2015).

Cultural, Ethnic, and Racial Justice
Cultural, ethnic, and racial justice examines racial and ethnic minority groups' experiences with the criminal justice system and the systemic ways in which racism and other forms of discrimination impact these groups (Department of Justice, 2019). This area seeks to understand the interplay between the criminal justice system, culture, ethnicity, and race and how it influences the experiences and outcomes for people of color. It also examines the policies, practices, and institutions that contribute to, perpetuate, or mitigate inequality and discrimination within the criminal justice system (Department of Justice, 2019).

Gender Justice
Gender justice looks at the ways in which our criminal justice system has systematically affected and continues to affect individuals based on their gender (Department of Justice, 2019). This area focuses on the intersecting factors of gender, race

Is downloading from torrent sites illegal in the USA?
Is it safe to download torrents from public trackers?
Is it safe to download torrents from private trackers?
Can I use a VPN to hide my IP address when downloading torrents?
Can I use a proxy to download torrents anonymously?
Is downloading copyrighted material illegal?
The legal landscape surrounding torrenting is a bit hazy in the United States, but there are some clear boundaries. The Digital Millennium Copyright Act of 1998 (DMCA) makes it illegal to "make available" or "make accessible to the public" copyrighted works without the copyright holder's permission. The DMCA also prohibits circumvention of technological measures that restrict access to copyrighted works. However, the DMCA does not make it illegal to use torrents to download copyrighted materials, as long as one does not violate the copyright owner's exclusive rights.

That being said, illegal torrenting is not a victimless crime. Torrenting can lead to malware infections, distributed denial of service (DDOS) attacks, and other types of cyber attacks. Torrenting also puts a massive strain on hosting servers and can cause them to crash.

It took three overtimes, but No. 16 BYU defeated Liberty 75-70 in the NCAA tournament’s first triple-overtime game on Thursday night in Salt Lake City.
The game was tied at 69-69 after two overtimes, but BYU’s Matt Haarms blocked Liberty’s Darius McGill’s shot in the third overtime, causing a riot on the Liberty bench.

text:
Negative Pneumatic Toothed Clutch with a Diaphragm Spring and a Roller and Spring Actuated Release Mechanism. In operation the friction surface of the clutches diaphragm is pressed against a plate by a roller and spring actuation with a certain leverage. This pressure causes a release of the clutches.
The Roller and spring actuated release mechanism is especially suitable where high torque is to be transmited between the clutch and the input shaft of the gearbox while also requiring a low release force. In this design the roller moves an engaging toothed plate connected to the input shaft and the spring presses the diaphragms against the plate engaging the clutches. This release mechanism avoids the use of a separate release pedal, enabling simpler and cheaper designs of clutch systems.
The release force is adjustable by moving the roller and thus the leverage. The force can be adjusted by changing the spring constant. A release force in the range of 5-15 N can be achieved.

Bob left Fort Cobb as a teenager and headed to Vancouver, B.C. He found work as a ship fitter and enjoyed the urban lifestyle of the busy seaport.
Bob married Joan and they settled in Vancouver, raising a family. He worked in construction and later as a machinist at Boeing, where he spent 24 years. When Boeing moved their operations to the U.S., Bob, at the age of 53, retrained and became a machinist for Canadian National Railways. He completed his career at the age of 78 working as a machine tender at the North Vancouver rail yards.
Bob served in the Royal Canadian Navy Reserve from 1950 to 1964 in various capacities including as an instructor and recruiter. He also served in the City of Vancouver Police Reserve and in the United Fire Fighters Association in Vancouver.

1. Changed by pressure so as to be deformed, usually permanently: an elastic material.
2. Elastic energy is the potential energy an object has because of its shape or configuration. In other words, it is the energy an object has when it is pushed or compressed.
Elastic materials tend to recover their original shape and size after being deformed or stressed. Examples of elastic materials are rubber, tubing, and foam.
Non-Elastic materials, on the other hand, do not return to their original shape once deformed beyond their allowable limits. Examples of non-elastic materials are brick, concrete, and glass.
The most common form of energy is elastic energy.
