text:
WBS: why is it important & why it can be difficult to estimate?
To add some extra context, WBS stands for Work Breakdown Structure. In project management, the WBS is a high-level, hierarchical decomposition of the work to be executed. The goal of creating a WBS is to facilitate project planning and management by breaking the project down into smaller, manageable units. This makes it easier to estimate the effort, resources, and time required for each unit of work.

Here are some reasons why the WBS is important:

1. **Effort estimation:** The WBS makes it easier to estimate the effort required for each unit of work. This is because it breaks down the project into smaller, manageable pieces, which makes it easier to understand the scope and complexity of each task.
2. **Resource allocation:** The WBS helps to allocate resources efficiently by identifying the specific tasks that require the most resources. This information can be used to make informed decisions about which tasks to prioritize and which resources to assign.
3. **Control and tracking:** The WBS can help to monitor progress and ensure that the project stays on track. It allows project managers to see at a glance what has been accomplished and what remains to

The Western Shoshone call themselves Newe, which means "the people". Like the Western Shoshone, the Eastern Shoshone refer to themselves as "the real people" (Nakaya, 1992).
One of the Eastern Shoshone's most important religious leaders was Chief Washakie, whose leadership was responsible for preserving and protecting the Shoshone’s traditional way of life.
Eastern Shoshone traditional ceremonies include the Sun Dance, a sacred rite of passage that has been passed down through the generations for centuries. The ceremony involves tying a pole in the centre of a sacred dance ground, usually on a high and visible location, for months and months to build up the sacred energy that will eventually take the dancers on this intense and spiritually cleansing ceremony (Eastern Shoshone Tribe, n.d.). According to one account, the Sun Dance ritual has been going on in the Wind River Mountains for over one thousand years (Eastern Shoshone Tribe, n.d.).

Anyone who has done a quick search on what to do when someone you know and love is experiencing a mental health crisis will have found out that you should never, ever leave them on their own. In fact, the recommendation in many sources is that they should be surrounded by support – family, friends, a mental health professional — all of whom are available to help when needed.
And yet, this isn’t easy. Even in this “supportive” age, mental health issues continue to be shrouded in shame and misconceptions, and family and friends can be as mystified and scared by the mental health crisis they are facing as the person themselves.
The mental health charity MQ has recently published a report calling for an increase in mental health support, and it’s hard to disagree. But the practical difficulties of finding support to help someone through a mental health crisis remain as huge as ever.
I know this because I’ve been working hard to help my partner (let’s call her Emma) over the last few months to navigate a series of mental health crises, ranging from depression and anxiety through to low-level psychosis. In recent weeks, her psychosis has led her to seek out and embrace Christian beliefs.

The worlds of ancient finance and cryptocurrency have continually been intertwined. Although they share some similarities, traditional and decentralised finance have their advantages and disadvantages. In essence, traditional finance has a long history, while cryptocurrencies offer unique features and benefits.
This article focuses on ancient financial and cryptocurrency systems in ancient Rome and Bitcoin's unique features to illustrate their significant and comparative benefits.
Ancient Rome maintained a highly complex financial system throughout its existence. This financial system included both public and private institutions that offered various financial services and products. Some of these services and products include depository and payment services, loans, investments, and investments in land and property.
Several ancient Roman financial institutions, both public and private, played essential roles in the day-to-day economy during Roman times. These institutions contributed towards Roman economic development, wealth distribution, and social stability.
The Roman State: The Roman State maintained considerable control over Roman finances by being responsible for creating and enforcing the Roman currency, the aes signum, which the ancient Romans called the "money of the people." The Roman State also provided deposit and payment services directly to the Roman public through the curranet, an official Roman banking system.

text:
It was time to pack up camp and head north.
We hit the road in the mist as the morning fog hung heavy over the highway. The first stop in our journey north is the beautiful Lake Tahoe.
Lake Tahoe, the largest alpine lake in North America and a popular spot for summer and winter recreation.
While the water wasn’t as crystal clear blue as I remembered, there was still a striking beauty to it.
Next stop was a brief visit to the historic Victorian town of Virginia City.
Historic store fronts, gold mining artifacts, the Miners Foundry Opera House, and the Montana Bar all contributed to making this a great place to spend an afternoon.
I had hoped to check out the Comstock Lode, which is believed to have produced more silver than any other mine in U.S. history, but weather conditions forced us to continue on to Salt Lake City.
Our journey took us through several mountain passes, past waterfalls and through scenic valleys. We spent the night in Evanston, Wyoming.
After a good night’s sleep, we headed east through the valleys of Grand Valley and out of the Wind River Mountains towards the Great Salt Lake.

If data can be HACKED or STOLEN, it can also be MISUSED or ABUSED – leading to serious harm to individuals, corporations, and nations. And, just as the potential risks of unauthorized access to data have been a consistent feature of the media and government reports for many years, so too have been reports of misuse and abuse of that data.
Data is not just numbers, it is not just facts, and it is not just information. It is the raw material out of which knowledge and insight are forged. It represents the stories we tell about ourselves and our world. While in some ways it is more accessible and more powerful than ever before, it is also more vulnerable. And the risks that come with that accessibility and power have never been greater, or more complex.
Privacy is not just a cultural or legal construct – though it is those things, too. It is a fundamental human right; a right that is recognized by the United Nations, and that is enshrined in the EU’s Charter of Fundamental Rights as one of the inherent human rights that every person is entitled to enjoy without distinction of any kind, such as race, colour, sex, language, religion, political or other opinion, national or social origin,

text:
Is an election recount a good idea?
The decision to request a recount in an election depends on the specifics of that election, including the margin of victory and the cost of the recount. If the difference between the winner and runner-up is less than one percent of the total votes cast, the candidates are usually required to pay the cost of the recount. However, recounts are not always successful in changing the outcome, and they may not necessarily be worth the cost. As a result, candidates have only a limited number of opportunities to request a recount.
In Wisconsin, for example, a candidate can request a recount if the candidate’s margin of victory is less than one percent, but the candidate must also pay the cost of the recount. Recounts are also allowed if there was fraud, misconduct, or other irregularities in the election.
In Minnesota, a candidate can request a recount if their margin of victory is less than one percent, or if they received fewer votes than the number of votes they were awarded by the voting machine tally. The cost of the recount is usually split evenly between the candidates. In Washington, a candidate must pay the full cost of the recount if their margin of victory is less than one percent. If their margin is greater than

Mamberamo River is the life line of West Papua, one of the biggest in Oceania and the third largest freshwater river in the world. The mighty river flows between steep valleys and is a haven for avid canoeists and rafters. The Mamberamo has a series of class I – IV rapids, and experienced rafters can also undertake a full day (16 miles or 26 Km) trip. For canoeists, the more mild class I – III rapids are easily manageable. If you are interested in the whitewater experience, Mamberamo is the longest whitewater run in the world. If you prefer to keep your feet dry, canoe trips on the Mamberamo River provide a more leisurely alternative. The river is surrounded by jungle and is also a great place for fishing.

Sugar consumption in North America is a huge concern and is currently driving the conversation around childhood obesity and other health conditions. Here’s what you need to know about sugar, children, and health.
Sugar, especially the added sugar found in candy, desserts, and processed foods, is a major contributor to the problem of childhood obesity. In fact, studies show that when children eat sugar-sweetened drinks, snacks or desserts, in addition to consuming a healthy diet, they gain more weight, and maintain a higher body mass index (BMI) over time when compared to kids who don’t consume sugar. The American Heart Association recommends that kids consume less than 25 grams a day (that’s about 6 teaspoons), while the World Health Organization has set the daily limit at 5% of total caloric intake.
According to a Centers for Disease Control report, children in the United States consume about 22 teaspoons of added sugar daily, more than three times the recommended amount. To put that in perspective, an 18-ounce soda contains about 9 teaspoons of sugar, and a regular-sized bagel has about 5 teaspoons of sugar.

A simple framework for developing a concept beneficiation plant. Mineral Processing and Extractive Metallurgy Review. 40(6):413-426.
Lack of data and understanding of the complex processes occurring within ore is a major hurdle in the design of new beneficiation plants for the upgrading of metalliferous ores. In order to effectively design a beneficiation plant, a tremendous amount of data and information needs to be gathered and analyzed to gain a better understanding of the ore body and the mineral processing options available. The data gathering process includes site surveys, logging of drill core, physical and chemical analysis, metallurgical testing, and interpretation and integration of data. The data is used to define size classes, produce mineralogical maps, develop process flow charts, and determine flow sheet strategies. This paper describes a simple framework for developing a concept beneficiation plant. This framework includes a list of tasks that may be used to organize the activities required for the development of a concept beneficiation plant. This framework provides a structured approach that will help identify problems, opportunities, and constraints within the mineral processing options available.

text:
I was brimming with excitement all of Thursday. When the day finally arrived for the start of the new school term, I was eager to learn new things in all my subjects. I was so eager in fact that I made the mistake of waking up to early. I stumbled out of bed, barely able to see straight, and rushed through my morning routine. By the time I made it to school, my brain had not had much of a chance to wake up, and my mind was not fresh or alert. I found it difficult to think and concentrate, and I quickly became overwhelmed by my classes as a result.
I've learned my lesson from that experience: It's always best to give yourself a little extra time in the morning to help your brain wake up and to prepare for the school day. After that, things went much smoother for me. I found that giving myself an extra 10-15 minutes each morning has made a big difference in how I feel throughout the day.
We've all experienced that feeling of rushing in the morning, and for many of us, it leads to a less than ideal start to the day. The effects of this can spill over into other parts of our lives, making us feel tired and unfocused at other times during
prompt_prefix: I was brimming with excitement all of Thursday.

text:
I figured if I could do it at all, it would be this day.
So I started out early today--about 11. In the summer, that is already late in the day here. Today was cooler than usual, though. It was only about 73 degrees when I started out, and there was a slight breeze.
Today's route was the same as one I have done before. It starts out at a road called Lime Kiln, which is a gravel road. From there I go through a canyon to the reservoir. Along the way, there are usually one or two cows to climb over, and sometimes more. The rocks are loose in places, and they are slippery when there is dew on them. There are some hills and switchbacks, too.
I was kind of feeling my way along the route. When I start out in the morning, I don't know exactly where I am going. I just sort of climb and look around. By the end of the route, it usually becomes clear where I have been.
As I climbed today, it became clear that not long ago I had climbed this particular stretch of road. In fact, I could almost see the exact spot I had climbed over the rocks and into the canyon

text:
Caused by pituitary adenomas, ischemic necrosis, cavernous hemangiomas or metastases are characterized by their rapid growth and large size. The aim of treatment is to remove the lesion, relieve compression of adjacent structures and to prevent recurrence. Endocrine lesions often necessitate preoperative localization and functional testing before surgery.
Transsphenoidal surgery is the treatment of choice for patients with acromegaly and Cushing’s disease caused by pituitary adenomas. This minimally invasive neurosurgical procedure allows for resection of lesions through a limited sublabial incision. The procedure is performed in an operating microscope that enables the dissection of the surgical field in a three-dimensional manner. The surgery has a high success rate and a low risk of postoperative bleeding and infections.
The treatment of cavernous hemangiomas and ischemic necrosis involves surgical removal of the lesions followed by radiation therapy for recurrent tumors. Metastatic spread of hormone-secreting pituitary tumors leads to tumor expansion and destruction of adjacent structures. Patients are monitored with imaging studies and biochemical testing. Surgery is indicated for symptomatic enlarged lesions or tumors causing invasion of adjacent structures. Adjuvant radiation therapy may be indicated for residual or recurrent disease. Medical treatment with bromocriptine or lanreotide may be required for life-long management

It sits between the cellular modem module, and the main board; and it features a range of interfaces which allow you to send data from other devices on the bus to the modem to send out via the cellular network.
The board also features a range of digital I/O pins connected to the main processor via a dedicated I/O bus. These are great because you can use them to monitor other devices on the bus, or to drive devices on the bus from the processor – this is how you can read data from sensors, turn LEDs on and off, or even drive motors and relays.
The board is small, and sits over top of the cellular module, so there's no need to install or move any parts in order to fit it. It also sits nicely alongside the processor, making for a neat and orderly wiring harness, especially if several similar devices are used.

text:
Copyrighted works of writing, art, or music. And, the most common type of copyright is for written works.
When you create something original and express it in a tangible form, you automatically own the copyright in that content as soon as it is created (under copyright law, the moment the work is fixed in a tangible medium of expression is the “creation date” for copyright purposes, even if the work was conceived earlier).
Under the copyright law, the copyright owner has the exclusive right to reproduce (or authorize the reproduction of) the protected subject matter; prepare derivative works based upon the subject matter of the copyrighted work; create abridgments, adaptations, compositions, translations, or other new works from the subject matter, or any portion of it; and to display the copyrighted work publicly.
For instance, if you write a story, you automatically own the copyright, but the second you publish that story in a book or an electronic format, you gain added benefits. Your work is then eligible to be registered with the copyright office and provides the author with certain legal and financial protections that can keep others from infringing, or using, your work without your permission. This type of protections usually come into play when someone does an unauthorized act of copying, distributing or displaying

Lippincott Company for the publication of Lewis' first Christian science fiction novel, The Screwtape Letters. The contract, executed by Lewis on December 11, 1950, is written on two pages, measures 8 x 11 inches, and is in excellent-minus condition with some soiling / toning to the outer edges; there is no noticeable writing or damage to the signatures. The agreement was made to produce twelve copies of the first edition of "The Screwtape Letters," with a printing cost to not exceed $1,500. The contract specifies that the author is entitled to $75 on the first 1,000 copies sold, $.50 on the first 10,000 copies sold, and $.25 on every 1,000 copies sold thereafter; they also agreed to pay Lewis a royalty of 25% of the retail price of each copy sold in their first five years of production. Lewis was paid $475 cash upon signing this contract, with the remaining royalties to be paid as stipulated in the document.

Children learn by watching and copying so model the correct way your child should hold their pencil or crayon while writing.
Here's some tips on modeling proper letter formation.
To create an uppercase "O," start at the top and make a slightly rounded line, stopping just short of the bottom.
For a lowercase "o," start at the top and make a small diagonal line to the right. Then, make a slightly rounded line going back downward, stopping just before touching the top line.
It can be helpful to show a child a correct and incorrect example of letter formation.

Bitcoin is a currency, used to exchange goods and services. In other words, Bitcoins are money and that’s essentially their main function. They're not a store of value, but a medium of exchange, like dollars or euros.
So Bitcoin can’t directly serve as the foundation for a new monetary system that’s stable and reliable. Instead, new systems designed specifically for that purpose will likely emerge and gain wider adoption. However, Bitcoin can continue to play an important role in the ecosystem of money because it’s digital, decentralized, and scarce.
To answer your question more directly: In the long term, Bitcoin has the potential to be a powerful medium of exchange, store of value, and unit of account, but those roles are still largely theoretical at this point. And, like gold, Bitcoin has intrinsic value due to its scarcity and technical properties, which makes it an attractive store of value.

The construction of passive voice in English is very similar to that of Spanish. In both languages, it is formed by placing the verb in a passive form (often in the third person singular) and adding a passive voice particle or a noun phrase to indicate the theme (the subject) of the passive construction.

In passive voice in English, the verb is in its passive form (often the 3rd person singular past tense) and is followed by a passive voice particle such as "by the time" or "at the time," which indicates the agent or the doer of the action. The theme (the subject) is then indicated by a noun phrase, which can be a noun or a pronoun.

Here are some examples of passive voice in English:

* He was baked by John. (Subject: He, Verb: was baked, Agent: John)
* The book was written by Stephen King. (Subject:

[The "l'espéranco de La Comunedal" movement
(1922-1939)].

This movement, from Catalonia, arose in 1922 as a response to the
institution of a authoritarian regime and the loss of political
freedom. It was led in the beginning by Francisco Fernández-Sánchez,
Pablo Ruiz, and other young intellectuals such as José Calvo del
Río. Its members, known as "esperantists," were devoted to the
philosophy, literature, and language of Esperanto, an artificial
language concocted by L. L. Zamenhof in the late 19th century. Though
not as widespread among the general population, the movement enjoyed
some support and was involved in literary, educational, and social
activities. The organization maintained ties with similar movements in other European countries, and some members even went on to careers as
literary and political figures.
The "l'espéranco de La Comunedal" was one of many diverse and
often contradictory groups that emerged during the transition to
modernity, and its brief existence (1937) reflects the brief period of
political freedom and pluralism that preceded the

The following cases are distinguished in the text: the action
for the recovery of money only; the assumpsit; the action of
tort; the action on the case; the action upon a contract; and
the action for a breach of contract.

In all these cases the plaintiff may demand and may recover in
curro dicens, as well as in forma pietatis.

When the demand is made in curro dicens, the action is termed,
in Latin, a causa poena, or a tort.

When the demand is not made in curro dicens, and the defendant
has committed a positive act of wrong, the action is termed,
in Latin, a casus poenale, or a torture.

If, however, the defendant was without fault, and yet the
injury inflicted is one that necessarily involves pain and
suffering, there remains no cause of action in tort, and a
action upon a contract only can be maintained.

About the game: Pool (billiards) is an individual sport that combines precision, accuracy, and endurance. The object of the game is to pot, or knock in, the specified number of objects (balls) while maintaining a specific level of accuracy and skill. The game typically involves a pool table or 6-recessed table with 15-17 balls (one white cue ball, one black cue ball, one grey group, one solid blue group, one solid red group, a yellow group, one additional solid colored ball or the option to use striped or spotted balls) and two cue sticks.
One player, called 'the breaker', strikes the center spot, known as the 'spot' with their cue stick. The breaker has the option to ‘bank' (bang the ball with a slight angle to bank it into a pocket) or ‘jump' (lift the cue tip from the cue ball and drive the ball into a pocket with a solid contact) the cue ball off the table to score points. The first player to pot one specific group of balls loses by not potting it

Bride was great fun because there've long been a number of horror movie franchises where the monster turns out to be a woman. The original Bride of Frankenstein (1939) is a classic example, but there have been others, including Dracula's Daughter (1936), The Vampire Bat (1933), and Son of Dracula (1943). As long as the woman is given a backstory, she can turn out to be monstrous, and the audience can feel a sense of foreboding or pity.
It's interesting to compare Bride to one of the movies that came before it. The Vampire Bat (1933) begins with a group of explorers discovering a hidden temple. One of the explorers, a young scientist named Rains, finds that the mummy-like beings within the temple are actually vampires. When Rains extracts a sample of these vampires' blood, he becomes infected. As the vampires start making appearances in London, Rains goes in search of a cure. Eventually, the real vampires turn out to have been turned into vampires by one of their female members. It's a very similar concept to Bride of Frankenstein.

**History lesson time!**

GM crops initially emerged in the 1970s as an alternative to glyphosate-based weed killers, such as Roundup®. These GM ‘Roundup-Ready’ crops, engineered so that the plants could tolerate glyphosate herbicide, enabled farmers using this chemical to dramatically reduce tillage in their crop fields. This not only saved on labor costs but also meant that the soil held its moisture better, making it less susceptible to erosion. The ‘Roundup-Ready’ crop systems also led to decreased dependence on pesticides and increased yield stability. Additionally, these crops could be grown using fewer resources, and fewer fossil fuels were used in their development and transportation (Lebow and Svejda, 2007). GM crops have contributed immensely to the environmental and agronomic improvements of agricultural production (IPES-Food, 2019; Tilman et al., 2012).

## Query Performance Tuning {#query-performance-tuning}

* **SELECT** : Use the following format for a **SELECT** statement to ensure the best **Query Performance**:
	+ **Indexed Columns (IC)** : At least one column from the indexed table.
	+ **Order by Clauses (OCl)** : Ascending order, one or more columns.
	+ **Top Count (TCl)** : Positive integer or **STAR\_TRIGGER** (all).

Example:
```sql
SELECT a.name, b.name, * FROM table1 a
INNER JOIN table2 b
ON a.column1 = b.column1
ORDER BY a.column2 ASC, a.columnX ASC, b.columnX ASC
LIMIT 3;
```

The above example selects three rows with column1 matching, order by column2 in ascending order, and selects columns a.name and b.name, ordered by a.column2 and followed by a selection based on b.columnX ascending, then a final selection based on b.columnX ascending again. Limiting by

This research investigates the use of EO (Electro-Optical) communication and 5G communication using Software Defined Radio (SDR) to develop an Aerostatic Unmanned Photographic Vehicle (UPV) with Real-Time monitoring capabilities. The EO communication will be done through an Optical Camera and the 5G communication will be achieved through the SDR. Mulberries come off best where you live then as although similar they really differ quite dramatically from their namesake the raspberry.

The main difference between Mulberry and Raspberry is in the taste. Mulberry has a taste similar to a combination of blackberry and raspberry. While raspberry has a very distinct tart and tangy flavor.

Mulberry is also much smaller than a raspberry, and doesn’t have as many seeds.

To sum-up, Mulberry tastes more like a combination of Blackberry and Raspberry whereas Raspberry tastes quite distinct and tart.

I would suggest that the raspberry is much more popular and often featured in desserts, cakes, etc. where as the mulberry isn't very popular, and isn't often seen in Western countries.

Source: Did a project on the differences between Mulberry, Raspberry and Blackberry during my food technology A Level and did a trip to the UK during the Mulberry season to taste them for myself.

5, 4 and two... that all kids will know, we like to have as foundation. The ones we like a bit less: 1, 6 and 8. We just don't see the reason to learn them.

In the end, it's all a matter of personal preference and what you as a parent find important and interesting. The most important thing is that your child is having fun and learns something new.

That said, learning numbers higher than 8 is quite easy, since most numbers have a simple place value structure - just think of 12 as 10 and 2, and the thousands place as a group of 10 (100). Title: Do's and Don'ts for PMs of SDMD Projects

---

### Do's

1. **Maintain Open & Clear Communication:** Regularly communicate with all team members, stakeholders, and stakeholders to ensure a smooth project flow. Concrete methods could involve daily team meetings and weekly progress updates. Maintain strong documentation and keep all records updated.

2. **Be Proactive:** Proactively identify and address potential risks to the project. Maintain a risk log and have a plan for addressing each identified risk. Be sure to consider contingency options.

3. **Ensure Clear Sustainable Development Goals and Benefits:** Ensure the project aligns with SDG (Sustainable Development Goals), as well as benefits, with the proper justification.

4. **Assign a Project Manager:** The PM is crucial for leading the project team, managing project deliverables, and managing budgets and resources.

5. **Establish Project Milestones:** Clear and well-defined project milestones are necessary to track progress and ensure project success.

[K]hvach`vi has written two significant body of literature analyzing Georges Bataille and Denis Zelazny`s major works in terms of the concepts of excess, incest, and ritual. Titled "The Triumph of Ritualism," Volume I of Khvach`vi's analysis investigates Bataille and Zelazny's work within the framework of ritual practice. In Volume II, "The Sublime and the Abyss," the focus is on the relationship between excess, incest, and the creation of the sublime, while also exploring the role of the "never-ending present" and the eternal return. Here Khvach`vi continues his exploration of ritual and the subliminal, exploring how the themes of incest, the eternal return, and excess are interconnected in Bataille's and Zelazny's work, in order to show how they have influenced philosophical and literary discourse, as well as our cultural understanding of the sublime and the sacred.

Meg Whitenama born as Jennifer Stanbach in 1952 and married Steven Allen Jones. Whitenama is a famous American psychologist, political leader and activist. She was the second female governor in United States history, serving one term as governor of California from 17 January 2007 to January 2011. Whitenama is the first and to date, the only women that has held the office of Governor of California and the 44th and current Governor of California.
Whitenama attended the University of California, Los Angeles and received her bachelor's and doctorate degrees in social psychology, and later a PhD in psychology from the University of California, Berkeley. She was the Clare Boothe Luce Policy Expert in the Reagan administration, U.S. Assistant to the President for Women's Rights and Family Issues and the Chair of the National Women's Political Caucus.
Whitenama campaigned as the Democratic Party's candidate in the 2006 California gubernatorial recall election, seeking to replace then-Governor Arnold Schwarzenegger within the context of the third California gubernatorial recall election. She defeated Arnold Schwarzenegger and later her predecessor, Gray Davis, to become the first and to date, the only woman to occupy the office of governor in California

3. Minimize JavaScript and CSS: Minimize the amount of JavaScript and CSS by using a tool like UglifyJS or webpack, which will create more minimal versions of the code.

Mumps is a contagious viral disease caused by the mumps virus. The virus is transmitted via droplets from the nose or mouth of an infected person, and can also survive on surfaces for up to two hours. People with mumps are usually most infectious from about 12 days before to about 4 days after the onset of symptoms, which is when mumps virus particles are most abundant in saliva.
The mumps vaccine is one of the safe and effective immunizations recommended by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to prevent mumps, measles, and rubella (MMR). The MMR vaccine has been highly effective in reducing the global incidence of mumps. According to the World Health Organization, after the introduction of widespread MMR vaccination, the global incidence of mumps has decreased by more than 95%.
While there is no specific treatment for mumps, it is a self-limiting disease. Symptoms usually last about 2-3 weeks, although joint pain, fatigue, and other symptoms may persist longer. In rare cases, mumps can lead to complications such as orchitis (swelling of the testicles) and encephalitis (sw

You also used the fact that the total number of days between the present and 8 months ago is equal to the number of days between 8 months ago and the earliest possible birthdate. But using the formula n=(B-A+1) where n is the number of days between two dates and A and B are the dates in days. We can rewrite it as n=(B-A+1) = (8-(A+1)+1) = 8 - A = 8 - 60 + 1, and A = 60 days. Then to find B, plug this into the equation n=(B-A+1)= (B-A+1)^2: 8^(2) = 64, so that B = 1 year and 60 days. This means that, despite your attempts to obscure your calculation, the correct birthdate is (1 year, 60 days) ago.

BATANG ASFATA: Why are Indian schools losing the plot and our authorities not doing anything about it?
In a recent case in New Delhi, students at a private school in Delhi tore pages out of a Bible and passed them around, disrespecting religious beliefs. When a student complained, the school administration threatened the student's family. Despite complaints to the police, no action was taken, and the school remained open.
This is one of about 30 similar cases that have come to light recently, all involving religious insensitivity and the deliberate disrespect of religious beliefs. These incidents highlight a concerning pattern.
An online forum for the school community boasts of its focus on "value education" and a curriculum that "touches the souls" of students. However, what is evident from the recent cases is that this kind of education is often void of respect for religious and cultural diversity. Students appear to be oblivious to the harm they can cause when they disrespect religion.
Most of the affected students come from religious backgrounds, but this does not mean that their family's faith should be insulted or their deeply held beliefs dismissed as superstitious or backwards. When students participate in these actions, others around them also feel emboldened. We are all complicit

This resource type is intended to represent the whole lifecycle of an API, from design and definition to publishing and retiring. Operations are the primary means of affecting the state of an API, and are used to add, remove, or modify API resources, such as APIs, API versions, and API bindings. Operations are classified as either backend or frontend based on where they operate. Backend operations affect the configuration of the API management service itself, while frontend operations affect the configuration of a specific API being managed. This resource type also includes operations for retrieving various types of information about the API, such as its definition, existing instances, and policies.
To represent an API definition, the API resource provides properties for describing the functionality provided by the API, such as its resources, operations, and policies. The definition is intended to be machine-processable, allowing other tools or systems to understand and utilize the API specification without user intervention.

NASA is exploring the concept of space colonies, and these designers are crafting what those planets might look like.
Flynn imagines a “futurist” space colony that reflects a 1950s American Dream ethos. Kell envisions a post-scarcity society that has shed the limits enforced by scarce resources. Sjoholm imagines a terraforming effort resulting in a lush, tropical world. VanderWee proposes a living structure that looks like something out of Star Trek. Vallaine envisions a bioengineered environment in which the “biosphere” is preserved to create a unique planetary habitat.
The discussion also includes a look at the challenges posed by sending humans to another planet and how those challenges might relate to the design of a new home.

2nd Annual Summer Social in Bellingdam for Newly Weds David N. and Sarah D.
On August 13, 2022, Bellingham Firefighters Local 24 and their families gathered at the beautiful Bellingham Golf and Country Club for the 2nd Annual Summer Social to welcome newlyweds David N. and Sarah D. into the Firefighter family.
David and Sarah had visited the Firefighter’s Ball in December 2021 but weren’t able to attend due to David being in the hospital with COVID. The event was postponed multiple times due to COVID restrictions, but the couple was so touched by the support they received from the department and local community that they decided to join in the fun at the Summer Social. The event featured food, drink, raffles, and other fun activities, including a beautiful, festive table display for David and Sarah.
The Firefighters Ball, and the 2nd Annual Summer Social, were made possible by the hard work and generosity of the members of Bellingham Firefighters Local 24. Thank you for bringing your community together to celebrate the happiness of David and Sarah!

is likely used at the remote DNS resolver. The resolvisor is able to open TCP connections to the nameservers. However, it is possible that Socksend can be used to send UDP packets to the nameservers via the resolvisor (which doesn't open a TCP connection). For this you can try the Socksend command in a terminal with root privileges:
```
sudo nslookup -send <query> <nameserver>
```
Replace the domain name or the query term with the target you want to query. For example to send a DNS query for google.com via the resolvisor the command would be:
```
sudo nslookup -send google.com <8.8.8.8>
```
If you get a response in the terminal, the resolvisor is able to send/receive other DNS queries and UDP packets to/from the nameservers. The response will contain the A and MX records for the domain you queried.

If Socksend can't be used to send DNS queries, you should try a different method to test the resolvisor, such as port scanning or trying to open TCP connections to the nameservers directly (which would require changing firewall

(AP) | BURKE GREYNUESSE was about to throw baseball pitch for the Covington High School Tigers... when something strange, and potentially dangerous, happened.
On the mound, in the final playoff game of his high school career, Greynuesse’s pitch soared toward the plate, as it does hundreds of times each season. And then -- out of the corner of his eye -- he saw something unfamiliar: A red shirt.
The Red shirt, a symbol of the Indigenous Peoples march, was being worn by several protestors outside the ballpark. Despite having been told to stay away from the stadium by authorities, they’d managed to sneak in and set up shop in the seats below the press box. They were chanting and holding signs, urging the Tigers’ players, other spectators and the umpires to respect indigenous people and their treaty rights.
The scene forced Greynuesse, a member of the Cherokee tribe, to take a deep breath and think about the importance of sport and activism. He recalled the story of Jim Thorpe, who in 1912 risked his Olympic medals to fight for the rights of the Lakota people in South Dakota. A few weeks before the games, a group of Lak

5. The design will support different compression methods, allowing for efficient data transmission over a variety of networks.
6. The design will support different encoding methods, such as base64 and URL-safe base64, allowing for efficient data storage and handling.
7. The design will support different encryption methods, such as TLS (Transport Layer Security) and SSL (Secure Sockets Layer), allowing for secure data transmission over the network

A major challenge of writing a Wish List Manager app is ensuring the data you add or modify remains consistent across all forms, pages, and views. One essential aspect of maintaining data consistency is managing when the data changes by notifying other parts of the app to update themselves.

For many years, the way WishList Player performed Wish List Observer notifications was limiting. If you have a Wish List Player with a lot of Wish Lists, and you have multiple users interacting with the Wish List Manager, you might have noticed a significant performance issue caused by the single-threaded communication system. You might have noticed that when a user adds or modifies a Wish List, there appears to be a delay before it propagates throughout the app.

We have listened to our user feedback, and we are excited to announce that as part of our update from WishList Player 7 to 8, we have improved the Wish List Observer so that it works in a more concurrent and efficient manner:

WishListObserver is now multi-threaded and designed so that it works across threads and processes. This means that when a user adds or mod

HISTAMIM is one part memory palace – a training and system based on creating connected memories linked to a place, sound, image or gesture – and one part journal and log book for the user to keep a record of what they have learned. It is aimed at helping users learn more efficiently and effectively by teaching them a technique based on research-proven memory techniques.
HISTAMIM is a game-changer for language learners; it can dramatically improve results by helping users remember new vocabulary and language structures more effectively and efficiently. By integrating music, sound effects, images, and gestures, the system helps learners to better understand and remember words, phrases, and grammar points. It’s like using a virtual memory palace that helps learners associate new vocabulary and structures with familiar, enjoyable, and memorable stimuli.
Language learners have a unique advantage when it comes to studying and learning using the HISTAMIM system. Since language learning is a form of language acquisition, the same techniques that help children learn a first language can also be applied to learning additional languages. HISTAMIM capitalizes on the brain’s natural ability to memorize through association and helps learners to make connections between new knowledge and existing information.

That is an interesting interpretation of Kafka's concept of the 'meta-morphosis,' or the moment of transformation which occurs in the midst of an everyday, routine action, such as going to work, buying groceries, or taking a shower. The idea that the transformation would only happen during a blackout is an innovative viewpoint that could be explored further.

In Kafka's story "Metamorphosis," Gregor Samsa experiences a transformative event in the midst of his daily routine, transforming into a giant bug. Kafka implies that this transformation is a result of Gregor's internal struggles, rather than a random or supernatural event. In your fictional adaptation of Kafka's concept, you suggest that the transformation might not happen during a blackout, but only in the moment just before the lights come back on, symbolizing a moment of clarity, hope, and transformation amidst the chaos of daily life.

“Majin is just an exaggerative name meant from dragon quest”. Dragon quest monster was based on a pachisi board game from the 80’s. Not to mention that the main villians of the Dragon Quest series are dragons, not demons.

Here is a link, which debunks some common misconceptions, about the games: https://www.gameskinny.com/4jG8j - Some of these misnomers can be traced back to the translations of the titles in the mid ’90s and early ’00s. Others are just based on common misconceptions.

Majin is a term used to describe demonic or fiendish beings in Japanese fiction. Since dragon quest takes place in a different world than our own it stands to reason that it’s creatures of the night would be different too. This doesn’t make them an “alternate history”. It means they drew inspiration from existing concepts and ran with it.

So stop spreading misinformation, okay? The correct term for a dragon quest monster is just that: a dragon quest monster. You can refer to them as demonic creatures if you want, but using the term “majin” to refer to them is incorrect.

5% fee per lot: This represents only five cents of additional commission when you trade 1 lot of a particular currency pair. This is the default commission that most brokers charge for each trade, which has the highest impact on the overall cost. In some cases, brokers do not charge any commission for trading, for example, with the OANDA broker or with FXTM.
3% fee per lot on the second lot and 2% after that: In this example, you would pay an additional 3% of the spread for the second trade, and 2% after that. The spread is the difference between the buy and sell price of an asset. This type of commission structure has the least impact on the overall cost, since the fee is only applied to additional trades.
1% profit: This is a hypothetical example, but it highlights how different types of broker fees can impact trading profitability. In this example, without these fees, you would be able to make a profit of $10 per lot (5% of $100 transaction cost and $100 profit). With the 5% fee, you would need a profit of $11.50 per lot to break even. With the 3% and 2% fee structure, you would need even more profits per lot

The WELTRACK APPROLLOCK is also known as a 'lip' or 'release lever' because it utilizes a locking mechanism that allows divers to open the lock lever and gently slide it forward, exposing the scuba diving regulator's high-pressure port. [HEADER]
-------------------
This header file contains the functions used to communicate with the GRIP module from the raspberry pi. This includes the initialisation and deinitialisation of the I2C communication, and the reading and writing of data to and from the device.

[FUNCTIONS]
---------------

### InitGRIP
Initiates the I2C communication with the GRIP module on the specified address `address`. This should be called once before using any other function. `address` can be a 7 or 8 bit address.
```c
int InitGRIP(uint8_t address);
```
### DeinitGRIP
Deinitialises the I2C communication with the GRIP module. This should be called once after finishing with the GRIP module.
```c
int DeinitGRIP();
```
### ReadGRIP
reads a byte from the GRIP module. This function blocks until a new value is received, and returns the same value that was read.
```c
int ReadGRIP(uint8_t *val);
```

(CNN)-- We spend nearly eighty centuries in our lives-- about 30 percent of which is claimed by childhood, and who knows how much more by old age. If the typical American life expectancy, which has been steadily increasing since the 1800s, continues its upward surge, we'll all be around a long time.
The American Life in the Twentieth Century series, a 10-volume set edited by John Kenneth Scott and published in the 1960s, offered readers a concise overview of the past 100 years of American life. The books, each one dealing with a particular aspect or phase of American life during the 20th century, provided an in-depth analysis of the historical, political, economic, and social trends that shaped modern America.
As Americans continue their quest for the meaning of life, the study of the human life course-- the sequence of stages from birth to death-- sheds light on the myriad of issues we encounter in our lives. Social, family, and individual life-course theorists have produced a body of research that has informed many of our present-day understandings of people and their lives.

As a rule, the system automatically checks for updates on the second startup after the computer is turned on. This is enabled by default, but it can be turned off in the system's configuration settings. Also, the updates can be installed manually by going to the "Windows Update" section of the Control Panel.

To turn off the automatic checking, follow these steps:

    Click Start, then click Control Panel.
    Click System, then click the "Advanced" tab.
    Under the "Startup and Recovery" section, uncheck the "Automatically restart" box and uncheck the "Write an event to the system log" box, or click the Edit button and clear the boxes. Click OK.
    Click Settings, then click Boot.
    Under the "System Startup" section, uncheck the "Automatic repair"

```yaml
apiVersion: operator.tigera.io/v1

  name: custom-argo-rollouts
  namespace: my-namespace

    version: v1alpha1
```

&gt& It appears (I cannot provide a screenshot to confirm right before being fired), that we've received an increase to the amount of gold required to advance from level 1 to level 2 by an order of magnitude, from 60 gold to 600 gold. The game didn't update the number of gold required, but as you can see by the top row, I have 60 gold and need 600 gold to advance.

It sounds like the amount of gold required to advance from level 1 to level 2 has changed, but the in-game UI hasn't been updated to reflect the change. This is an issue with the game engine, rather than an issue with Overwolf. The developers of the game would need to update the UI to reflect the change in gold requirement. In the meantime, you can use a Fake Gold mod to display an inaccurate but smaller gold requirement until the developers make the changes officially.

If you suspect the mod is causing the issue, try removing it and see if the gold requirement in-game is corrected. You can also try verifying the game files through Steam to check for any corruption and see if that resolves the issue without requiring a mod.

- C-POPSHOT (http://c-popshot.tuxfamily.org/)
- Poplib (http://www.poplib.co.uk/)
- SpamAssassin/Postfix (with external SMTP server, if required)
- OpenPGP/GPG (with external SMTP server)

Using any of these products will allow you to implement any email functionality that you need, without running a compromised server.

Horseshit....you got some Horseshit!” So how exactly were John Howard and Tony Abbott preparing the nation for change when they were elected to the office of Prime Minister in 1996 and 2013 respectively? The answer lies in the strategies employed, in the tools used and in the messages conveyed to the Australian people.
John Howard, in his 1996 campaign, was clear in his mantra that “there you are, the status quo, it’s worked for a hundred years or more and it’s clearly not going to change.” The message was simple – if you vote for us, we’re going to keep things the same. The government and its agencies would keep doing what they were doing, there would be no big reforms and no new programs. No radical change was on the cards and that was the way it should be. Australians were generally comfortable with his message, a fact that saw the government retain power for twelve years.
But then Tony Abbott came along with his own, slightly different approach. From the very first day he took charge, Abbott signaled that things were going to change – a lot. He promised to reverse Labor’s spending, to abolish job-destroying Labor legislation, and to abolish or dramatically reduce other unwanted programs

That's *excusable.* That's like an NBA team putting up 20 points a game - I mean, who cares about the NBA? I'd have way less fun watching that than I would watching a team lose 6-0.

In fact, that'd be the *second* worst winning percentage in NFL history - and it wouldn't be close. They'd have a 0.0% winning percentage, which has only been done once in NFL history. The only teams with worse winning percentages than 0.0% have never even played in the NFL: they've played in the AAFC, an entire league that lasted from 1946-49, before even getting to the NFL. One team finished 0-7-1.

So, yeah. That's *excusable.* That's like the NFL team getting clobbered in a high school basketball game - that's just not something the NFL team is expecting. They'd expect to get worked in basketball from a high school team; they absolutely should not expect it in football, especially not against *another NFL team.*

M.S.-Brief The New England journal offers numerous studies showing a direct connection between low back pain and sitting. Sitting is actually hurting people, even if they aren??™t aware of it and even if they think they are engaged in some other activity.

Discussion:
A review of the literature, including a comprehensive analysis of relevant studies and data over the past decade, has determined that prolonged sitting leads to poor posture, increased lumbar flexion, diminished hip and trunk extensor strength, and neuromuscular adjustments to maintain neutral lumbar posture and to prevent low back pain. However, prolonged sitting disrupts the neuromuscular system, which reduces the time spent in stable muscle activity necessary to maintain neutral lumbar spine position and increases the likelihood of lumbopelvic instability and pain. (1) Consequently, we propose that a sedentary lifestyle in which individuals spend a large portion of the day in a seated position, especially those with lumbopelvic instability, is an important risk factor for low back pain.

Instance segmentation involves assigning a unique identifier (Classification) to every object or instance (Detected Face) present in an image or video frame. Segmentation masks are derived from instance segmentation annotations by drawing bounding boxes around these object instances and then masking out everything outside these boxes. Instance segmentation is very useful in applications like object detection, tracking, and computer vision. The RICHLab instance segmentation pipeline involves using object detection and instance segmentation algorithms to generate instance segmentation masks that cover individual objects in an image or video. These masks can then be used to identify the objects and extract relevant features for computer vision tasks like image classification and object detection. The following sections will walk through the basic step-by-step method of creating instance segmentation masks using the RICHLab instance segmentation pipeline.

Step 1: Object Detection

CurlUp allows patients who need durable insertion supplies or additional devices due to injury or ongoing treatment to receive those through home delivery instead of traveling to physical locations. Our mission is to elevate the standards of healthcare delivery for wound care and ostomy patients by providing education, resources, and home delivery services that empower and support our customers’ health and well-being.

### Who are CurlUp's customers?

CurlUp serves patients who have been prescribed or recommended a wound care or ostomy product by a healthcare provider for the management of their condition. Patients may receive durable insertion supplies or additional devices due to injury or ongoing treatment. Our focus is on patients with chronic illness or injuries, but people with temporary conditions or those who have recently undergone surgery can also benefit from our products and services.

### What markets does CurlUp serve?

CurlUp serves customers in the continental United States.

### Can anyone order from CurlUp?

No. CurlUp is designed for those who have been prescribed or recommended a wound care or ostomy product by a healthcare provider for the management of their condition. If you are unsure if CurlUp is right for you, please consult with your healthcare provider.

Mesocuffocalli syndrome, an acaroangist eosinophobia usually produced a papular and vesicular rash, as well as fever and eosinophilia associated with ingestion of insects, mites or their exosomns. In areas where mites are more common, such as southern USA and Europe, mesocuffocalli syndrome is a well-known entity reported in several cases in the literature. In this report, we present a case of Mesocuffocalli syndrome confirmed by biopsy and direct immunofluorescence in a 46 year-old female patient who presents erythematous plaques, scarlatiniform rash along her extensor surfaces as well as eosinophilia and positive direct immunofluorescence test for Dermatophagoides farinae, a common mite species in USA.
A case of Mesocuffocalli syndrome is presented who was diagnosed based on clinical presentation, biopsy and direct immunofluorescence examination of skin specimens. To our knowledge, this is the first reported case of this syndrome in the medical literature, in a patient from Colombia, a country with a different geography from the traditional areas of mesocuffocalli syndrome.

Knee arthoscopy (keys-yard surgery - named of Mr Patrick Arger's invention back in the early 20th century), and the most common knee arthroscopy procedure is knee meniscal tear repair. The meniscus is a fibrous tissue, like gored lamb's skin, that stabilizes the knee joint. Most meniscus tears occur with mechanical stress, such as pivoting, cutting, turning or in older age, with degenerative changes. Meniscal tears can be classified as either degenerative (degenerative meniscus) or traumatic (acute meniscal tear) (1).

After the arthroscopic examination of the knee, a full thickness meniscal tear (over 50% of the thickness) is considered a degenerative meniscal tear (2,3; Grade 2 or 3 defects in Outerbridge classification; Fig. 1). The meniscus is a fibrocartilage tissue and it loses water content with age, depleting the tissue's elastic properties and consequently compromising its healing potential (4; Fig. 1). Defects >50% can lead to a significant loss of articular surface, causing joint discomfort and osteoarthritis if untreated (5; Fig. 1)

im Jahre 1894 von Adolf Heiting, dem Sohn des Autors, im Wohnhaus in Eisfeld,
Sudderniedersachsen, gezeigt. Im Kopfzeile steht:

Heidingauer Korrespondenz 1894

Im Zweiten Teil, die Korrespondenz nach den Tagen des Heidingaus, das
nachgesagt wird, wird der "Tagebuch" vergrößert und in seine Einzelhender
gezählt. Im Kopfzeile steht immer noch:
Heidingauer Korrespondenz 1894

Tagesperiode enthalten und sieht im Vergleich zum Tagebuch äußer

One of the earliest printers utilizing type-list technology is described in the paper "Rearrangable Type-List Printers with Automatic Space Reduction" by LaVelle Sund. In the 1960s, when typewriters were still the primary means of writing, printers were expensive and cumbersome devices.

Sund proposed a new printer utilizing type-list technology, which would produce readable text by combining the symbols of individual typebars to form complete characters. He introduced four types of type-lists to support these characters:

1. Common type-lists: Common type-lists contained the characters that appeared most often in English words. By reordering these characters and combining a few of the most common characters, space savings of up to 75% could be achieved.
2. Character type-lists: Character type-lists were a set of standard characters with spaces inserted in between. These type-lists provided another space-saving mechanism, with potential savings up to 50% over common type-lists.
